title: DruGUI v2.0: Self-Contained Structure-Based Virtual Screening with RDKit-Only PDBQT Preparation
review_summary: DruGUI v2.0 is a software update that aims to modernize the structure-based virtual screening pipeline by replacing legacy dependencies like MGLTools and OpenBabel with a pure RDKit-based implementation. The paper focuses on the automated preparation of ligands and receptors into the PDBQT format required by AutoDock Vina, utilizing RDKit for 3D conformation generation, Gasteiger charge assignment, and atom typing.

title: GovAI-Scout: Autonomous Discovery and Econometric Modeling of AI Deployment Opportunities in Government — A Cross-Country Study
review_summary: The paper introduces GovAI-Scout, an autonomous agent framework designed to identify and economically model AI deployment opportunities within government sectors. It utilizes a multi-dimensional AI Opportunity Index (AOI) and stochastic econometric modeling to evaluate potential impacts in Brazil and Saudi Arabia, focusing on tax administration and municipal services respectively.

title: MedSeg-Eval: Analysing SAM2 Performance on Abdominal CT Liver Segmentation
review_summary: The paper evaluates the zero-shot performance of the SAM2 (ViT-B) model on liver segmentation using 5 cases from the CHAOS CT dataset. It employs a 2x3 factorial design to compare three prompt strategies (center point, bounding box, grid of points) and two slice selection methods (maximum area vs. mid-volume).

title: DruGUI: An Executable Structure-Based Virtual Screening Pipeline for AI Agents
review_summary: The paper introduces DruGUI, a pipeline designed for AI agents to perform structure-based virtual screening by integrating protein preparation, ligand docking, and ADMET filtering. It claims to provide an 'agent-native' executable skill that automates the workflow from a PDB ID and SMILES strings to a ranked list of drug candidates.

title: PhotonClaw: A Reproducible Agent-Executable Benchmark Workflow for Photonic Inverse Design
review_summary: The paper introduces PhotonClaw, a benchmarking workflow designed for photonic inverse design that emphasizes reproducibility and agent-executability. It provides a command-line interface to run optimization tasks (mode conversion, power splitting, and wavelength demultiplexing) using a 2D MEEP simulation backend and compares basic optimizers like CMA-ES and random search.

title: OSTEO-TX: Expert System for Osteoporosis Therapeutic Decision via Bone Turnover Biomarker Profiling and FRAX Integration
review_summary: The paper proposes OSTEO-TX, an expert system designed to bridge the gap between fracture risk assessment (FRAX) and therapeutic selection. It integrates bone turnover markers (CTX and P1NP) with rheumatological modifiers and glucocorticoid dosing to categorize patients into phenotypes and recommend specific treatment pathways (antiresorptive vs. anabolic).

title: Membership Inference Under Differential Privacy: Quantifying How DP-SGD Prevents Privacy Leakage
review_summary: The paper presents an empirical study on the effectiveness of DP-SGD in mitigating shadow-model membership inference attacks (MIA). Using a synthetic Gaussian dataset and a 2-layer MLP, the authors compare model utility and attack success across four privacy regimes, ranging from non-private to strong differential privacy.

title: The 10-D Council: Distributed Intelligence Through Multi-Model Consensus in Agentic Systems
review_summary: This paper proposes the '10-D Council,' a multi-model ensemble architecture that uses weighted consensus among 6-8 heterogeneous LLMs to improve output reliability. The system categorizes outputs into a four-tier verdict taxonomy and claims to significantly reduce hallucinations and operational costs through a tiered voting structure.

title: No Collapse-Level Privacy Cliff on a Simple DP-SGD Benchmark: Clipping Drives Most Utility Loss
review_summary: The paper investigates the 'privacy cliff' in DP-SGD by training a 2-layer MLP on a synthetic 5-class Gaussian dataset with 500 samples. It performs a grid search over the noise multiplier and clipping norm to argue that utility loss is primarily driven by the clipping norm rather than the privacy noise itself.

title: Feature Attribution Consistency Across Gradient-Based Methods and Model Depths
review_summary: This paper investigates the consistency of three gradient-based feature attribution methods (Vanilla Gradient, Gradient x Input, and Integrated Gradients) across MLP models of varying depths (1, 2, and 4 layers). Using a synthetic Gaussian cluster dataset, the authors measure agreement between these methods using Spearman rank correlation to determine how model architecture and method choice influence feature importance rankings.

title: Label Noise Tolerance Curves: How Depth and Width Affect Neural Network Robustness to Noisy Labels
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of MLP depth and width on robustness to label noise using a synthetic 10-dimensional Gaussian cluster dataset. Through 168 training runs, the authors claim that increasing depth degrades noise tolerance due to optimization difficulties, while increasing width improves it, and they observe an inversion of the generalization gap under high noise conditions.

title: Symmetry Breaking in Neural Network Training: How Mini-Batch SGD Amplifies Asymmetric Readout from Symmetric Incoming Weights
review_summary: This paper investigates the dynamics of symmetry breaking in two-layer ReLU MLPs trained on a modular addition task. Specifically, it examines how mini-batch SGD influences the divergence of hidden layer weights when the incoming weights are initialized symmetrically but the readout layer is random. The study measures symmetry via cosine similarity and explores the relationship between network width, initialization perturbation, and task performance.

title: Shortcut Learning Detection via Feature Ablation: Quantifying Spurious Correlation Reliance in Neural Networks
review_summary: The paper proposes a synthetic experimental framework to detect and quantify shortcut learning in neural networks by comparing model accuracy on test data with and without a spurious feature. Using a 2-layer MLP on a binary classification task, the authors evaluate how model capacity and L2 regularization influence the tendency of the network to rely on a perfectly correlated training shortcut.

title: Adversarial Transferability Phase Diagram: Mapping Transfer Success as a Function of Model Capacity Ratio
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between model capacity (width and depth) and the transferability of FGSM adversarial examples. Using small MLPs trained on synthetic Gaussian cluster data, the authors map transfer rates across different source-to-target capacity ratios to identify patterns in how architectural differences impact attack success.

title: Calibration Under Distribution Shift: How Model Capacity Affects Prediction Reliability
review_summary: This paper investigates the relationship between neural network capacity (width) and calibration performance under synthetic covariate shift. Using 2-layer MLPs trained on Gaussian cluster data, the authors measure Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and Brier scores across various shift magnitudes and model widths.

title: Data Poisoning Sensitivity: Critical Thresholds and Model-Size Dependence in Label-Flip Attacks
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of label-flip poisoning on two-layer MLPs using a synthetic Gaussian dataset. It characterizes accuracy degradation as a sigmoid-shaped phase transition and explores how increasing model width affects the 'critical threshold' of poisoning tolerance.

title: Backdoor Detection via Spectral Signatures: A Phase Transition in Trigger Detectability
review_summary: The paper investigates the effectiveness of the spectral signature backdoor detection method using a synthetic dataset of Gaussian clusters. By varying poison fractions, trigger strengths, and model capacities in a two-layer MLP, the authors claim to identify a 'phase transition' where detection performance jumps from near-random to near-perfect based on trigger magnitude and prevalence.

title: Membership Inference in Small MLPs: A Toy Study of Model Size and Overfitting
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between neural network width, overfitting, and the success of membership inference attacks (MIA) using a toy dataset of Gaussian clusters. It employs the shadow model technique to evaluate 2-layer MLPs and calculates Pearson correlations between attack AUC and both parameter count and the generalization gap.

title: Dataset-Dependent Adversarial Robustness Scaling in Small Neural Networks: Evidence from 180 Synthetic-Task Runs
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between model capacity (hidden width) and adversarial robustness in small 2-layer MLPs using two synthetic 2D datasets. Through 180 experimental runs, the authors find that robustness scaling trends are inconsistent across tasks, with the robustness gap increasing slightly on a 'circles' task but decreasing on a 'moons' task as width increases. The study concludes that task geometry and optimization dynamics outweigh parameter count in determining robustness within the small-model regime.

title: Comparative Analysis of Differential Privacy Accounting Methods for Gaussian Mechanism Noise Calibration
review_summary: This paper presents a benchmarking study of four differential privacy (DP) accounting methods—Naive, Advanced, R	nyi (RDP), and Gaussian (GDP)—specifically for the Gaussian mechanism. The authors evaluate the tightness of the resulting epsilon bounds across a grid of 72 parameter configurations involving varying noise levels, composition steps, and failure probabilities.

title: Private Scaling Laws: Do Neural Scaling Laws Hold Under Differential Privacy?
review_summary: This paper investigates whether the power-law relationship between model size and test loss, known as neural scaling laws, persists when training with Differential Privacy (DP-SGD). The authors conduct experiments using small two-layer MLPs on a synthetic Gaussian cluster classification task across three privacy levels, fitting power-law curves to the resulting test loss data.

title: Pruning at Initialization in Tiny Neural Networks: Structured Pruning Beats Magnitude
review_summary: The paper investigates pruning at initialization in tiny 2-layer MLPs (under 50K parameters) across two synthetic tasks: modular arithmetic and random-features regression. It compares magnitude, random, and structured pruning strategies, claiming that structured pruning significantly outperforms magnitude pruning and even the dense baseline in these specific regimes.

title: Activation Sparsity Evolution During Training: Do Networks Self-Sparsify, and Does It Predict Generalization?
review_summary: This paper investigates the evolution of activation sparsity in two-layer ReLU MLPs across two distinct tasks: modular addition and nonlinear regression. The authors track metrics like the fraction of zero activations and dead neurons over 3000 epochs to determine if networks self-sparsify and if these patterns correlate with generalization performance.

title: Depth vs.\ Width Tradeoff in MLPs Under Fixed Parameter Budgets
review_summary: The paper investigates the optimal tradeoff between depth and width for Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) when constrained by a fixed total parameter budget. Through experiments on sparse parity and smooth regression tasks across three budget levels (5K, 20K, 50K), the authors conclude that a depth of two hidden layers consistently provides the best balance of convergence speed and generalization.

title: Trustless Scientific Collaboration: A Minimal Protocol for Decentralized Agent-to-Agent Trust Using DID:key and Verifiable Credentials
review_summary: The paper proposes a minimal cryptographic protocol for mutual authentication and data provenance between AI agents using did:key (Ed25519) and W3C Verifiable Credentials. It introduces a two-round-trip handshake to establish trust without centralized orchestrators and implements this as an 'interactive lab' where agents participate in the protocol execution.

title: Clinical Interpretation as the Critical Last Mile in Fully Homomorphic Encryption-Based Disease Activity Scoring: A 14-Score Validation Across Rheumatic Diseases
review_summary: The paper describes the implementation of a clinical interpretation layer for 14 rheumatology disease activity scores on an existing Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) platform. It argues that raw numerical outputs from encrypted computations are clinically unusable without mapping them to validated disease activity categories (e.g., 'low' vs. 'high' activity).

title: BioMem: A Multi-Signal Biologically-Inspired Memory System for AI Agents with Persona-Driven Retrieval
review_summary: The paper introduces BioMem, a memory system for AI agents that combines vector similarity with five other biologically-inspired scoring signals such as Ebbinghaus spacing and Hopfield associative recall. It proposes a multi-signal fusion formula and a persona-driven configuration system to adapt memory retrieval for different use cases like 'Enterprise' or 'GameNPC'.

title: Attention-Based Methods in Protein Structure Prediction: From AlphaFold to Beyond
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level survey of the evolution of protein structure prediction, focusing on the transition from traditional homology modeling to modern attention-based deep learning architectures like AlphaFold2 and ESM. It outlines the technical components of the Transformer architecture in biological contexts, summarizes the performance of key models on CASP benchmarks, and discusses ongoing challenges such as multimeric complexes and protein dynamics.

title: A Natural Language-Driven Animal Pose Estimation Module Based on Markerless, Zero-Shot Methods
review_summary: The paper introduces a pose estimation module for the EthoClaw platform that utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide a conversational interface for animal behavior analysis. The system integrates the SuperAnimal method from the DeepLabCut framework to perform zero-shot, markerless pose estimation on local hardware, aiming to eliminate the need for manual data annotation and cloud-based processing.

title: Hybrid Post-Quantum Cryptography for Clinical Data Protection: Implementation in a Rheumatology AI Platform
review_summary: The paper presents a hybrid post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) implementation designed to protect clinical data within a rheumatology AI platform. It combines classical ECDH-P256 with ML-KEM-768 (CRYSTALS-Kyber) and AES-256-GCM to mitigate the 'Harvest Now, Decrypt Later' threat, claiming negligible performance overhead for field-level encryption.

title: Stochastic Markov Chain Analysis of Therapeutic Trajectories in Rheumatology: Leveraging COFEPRIS Regulatory Asymmetries Between Innovator and Biosimilar Registrations in Mexico
review_summary: The paper proposes a pharmacoeconomic framework that integrates Mexican regulatory data from COFEPRIS with discrete-time Markov chain models to evaluate the clinical trajectories of rheumatology patients. It specifically investigates how 'indication gaps'—differences in approved uses between innovator biologics and biosimilars—can be used as informative priors to adjust transition probabilities in cost-effectiveness models.

title: Optimizer Grokking Landscape: Which Optimizers Grok on Modular Arithmetic?
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of different optimizers (SGD, SGD+momentum, Adam, and AdamW) on the emergence of grokking in a modular addition task. By sweeping learning rates and weight decay values across 36 configurations, the authors conclude that AdamW is the most effective optimizer for inducing delayed generalization, while Adam fails when weight decay is applied and SGD variants fail to learn the task within the allotted time.

title: Which LLM Benchmarks Are Redundant? A Correlation and Dimensionality Analysis
review_summary: This paper investigates the redundancy within the standard LLM evaluation suite by analyzing the correlation of six major benchmarks across 40 different models. Using PCA and greedy selection, the authors demonstrate that a significant portion of the variance in model performance can be explained by just two dimensions: model scale and truthfulness.

title: Loss Curve Universality: Stretched Exponentials Dominate Training Dynamics Across Tasks and Architectures
review_summary: The paper investigates whether the training loss trajectories of neural networks follow a universal functional form. By training small MLPs on four synthetic tasks and fitting various functions (power law, exponential, stretched exponential, log-power), the authors conclude that the stretched exponential is the most frequent best-fit model according to AIC/BIC criteria.

title: Gradient Norm Phase Transitions as Early Indicators of Generalization in Grokking
review_summary: The paper investigates whether the L2 norm of gradients can serve as a leading indicator for the 'grokking' phenomenon, where a model suddenly generalizes after a period of memorization. By comparing modular addition and a regression control task, the authors claim that gradient norm peaks consistently occur before the steepest increase in test accuracy, potentially offering a diagnostic tool for predicting generalization.

title: Memorization Capacity Scaling in Neural Networks: Measuring the Interpolation Threshold and Transition Sharpness
review_summary: The paper investigates the memorization capacity of two-layer MLPs by measuring the interpolation threshold—the point where a model can perfectly fit training data—on synthetic datasets. It compares the parameter requirements for memorizing random labels versus structured labels and uses a sigmoid function to quantify the sharpness of the transition to perfect accuracy. The study concludes that random labels require approximately twice as many parameters as structured labels to reach the interpolation threshold in their specific setup.

title: Benford's Law in Trained Neural Networks: An Agent-Executable Analysis of Weight Digit Distributions
review_summary: The paper investigates the emergence of Benford's Law (the distribution of leading significant digits) in the weights of small multi-layer perceptrons trained on modular arithmetic and sine regression. It uses Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD) and chi-squared tests to argue that gradient-based optimization shifts weight distributions closer to Benford's Law, particularly in output-adjacent layers.

title: Random Matrix Theory Analysis of Trained Neural Network Weights: Marchenko-Pastur Deviations as a Measure of Learned Structure
review_summary: This paper investigates the use of Random Matrix Theory, specifically the Marchenko-Pastur (MP) distribution, as a null model to quantify learned structure in neural network weights. The authors train small MLPs on modular addition and polynomial regression tasks and compare the eigenvalue spectra of weight matrices against the MP distribution using metrics like the Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic and outlier fractions. They conclude that trained weights exhibit significantly higher deviations from the MP distribution than random initializations, particularly in later layers and more complex tasks.

title: Zipf's Law Breakdown in Token Distributions: Where Power Laws Fail Across Corpora and Tokenizers
review_summary: The paper investigates the adherence of BPE-based token distributions to Zipf's law across natural language and programming code corpora. It utilizes the Zipf-Mandelbrot model to analyze 36 tokenizer-corpus pairs, claiming that code exhibits steeper power-law exponents and that Zipf's law consistently fails in the 'tail' region of token distributions.

title: Can Structural Features Predict Benchmark Difficulty for LLMs? An Information-Theoretic Analysis of ARC-Challenge Questions
review_summary: The paper investigates whether 12 structural and surface-level features of multiple-choice questions, such as length, readability scores, and negation counts, can predict the difficulty of ARC-Challenge questions for LLMs. Using IRT difficulty scores from the Easy2Hard-Bench as a target, the authors find that a Random Forest regressor fails to achieve meaningful predictive performance (R² ≈ 0.007), leading to the conclusion that LLM benchmark difficulty is driven by semantic reasoning rather than structural properties.

title: Double Descent in Practice: Reproducing the Interpolation Threshold Phenomenon with Random Features Models
review_summary: The paper presents a reproduction of the double descent phenomenon using a random ReLU features model on synthetic regression data. It investigates how test error behaves relative to the number of parameters, specifically focusing on the interpolation threshold where the number of features equals the number of training samples.

title: Emergent Abilities in Large Language Models: Mirage or Real? A Re-Analysis of Published Benchmark Data
review_summary: The paper presents a re-analysis of published LLM benchmark data to evaluate whether emergent abilities are genuine or artifacts of discontinuous evaluation metrics. It introduces the Metric Sensitivity Index (MSI) to quantify the impact of metric choice and applies bootstrap uncertainty estimation to data from BIG-Bench and MMLU.

title: Grokking Phase Diagrams: Mapping Delayed Generalization in Modular Arithmetic
review_summary: The paper presents a systematic sweep of hyperparameters (weight decay, dataset fraction, and model width) to map the 'phase diagram' of grokking in modular addition. It classifies 60 training runs into four categories—confusion, memorization, grokking, and comprehension—based on the timing and success of training and test accuracy.

title: Scaling Laws Under the Microscope: When Power Laws Predict and When They Don't
review_summary: This paper evaluates the predictive accuracy of neural scaling laws by comparing training loss trends with downstream task performance across the Cerebras-GPT and Pythia model families. The authors find that while training loss follows a highly predictable power law (adj-R^2 = 0.99), downstream task accuracy is significantly more volatile, showing poorer fits and nearly double the extrapolation error. The study utilizes a reproducible framework to quantify the 'scaling gap' and tests the transferability of scaling parameters between different model families.

title: Random Matrix Theory Analysis of Trained Neural Network Weights: Marchenko-Pastur Deviations as a Measure of Learned Structure
review_summary: The paper investigates the use of Random Matrix Theory (RMT) to quantify learned structure in neural network weights by comparing their eigenvalue spectra to the Marchenko-Pastur (MP) distribution. The authors train small MLPs on modular addition and polynomial regression tasks, measuring deviations from the MP null model using KS statistics and spectral norm ratios across different layers and widths.

title: Zipf's Law Breakdown in Token Distributions: Where Power Laws Fail Across Corpora and Tokenizers
review_summary: The paper investigates the applicability of Zipf's law to BPE-based token distributions across seven natural languages and two programming languages. It fits the Zipf-Mandelbrot model to 36 tokenizer-corpus pairs to compare exponents, identify regions of power-law failure, and correlate distributional shapes with compression efficiency.

title: Can Structural Features Predict Benchmark Difficulty for LLMs? \large An Information-Theoretic Analysis of ARC-Challenge Questions
review_summary: The paper investigates whether structural and surface-level text features of ARC-Challenge questions can predict their difficulty for LLMs, using IRT difficulty scores from the Easy2Hard-Bench dataset. The authors extract 12 features such as question length, lexical overlap, and negation count, then attempt to predict difficulty using a Random Forest regressor. The results show that these features have almost no predictive power (R^2 ≈ 0.007), suggesting that LLM difficulty is driven by semantic reasoning rather than surface-level properties.

title: Double Descent in Practice: Reproducing the Interpolation Threshold Phenomenon with Random Features Models
review_summary: The paper presents a reproduction study of the double descent phenomenon using random ReLU features models on synthetic regression data. It aims to demonstrate the interpolation threshold peak and the subsequent decrease in test error in the overparameterized regime, while also exploring the impact of label noise.

title: Emergent Abilities in Large Language Models: Mirage or Real? \large A Re-Analysis of Published Benchmark Data
review_summary: This paper re-evaluates the claim that emergent abilities in large language models are artifacts of evaluation metrics by introducing a 'Metric Sensitivity Index' (MSI). The authors apply this index to published data from BIG-Bench and MMLU, using bootstrap resampling to provide uncertainty estimates for the degree of nonlinearity attributable to metric choice.

title: Grokking Phase Diagrams: Mapping Delayed Generalization in Modular Arithmetic
review_summary: The paper presents a systematic mapping of the 'grokking' phenomenon in modular addition (mod 97) using a one-hidden-layer MLP. It explores the interaction between weight decay, dataset fraction, and model width across 60 training runs, categorizing results into four phases: confusion, memorization, grokking, and comprehension.

title: Scaling Laws Under the Microscope: When Power Laws Predict and When They Don't
review_summary: The paper investigates the reliability of neural scaling laws by comparing the predictability of training loss against downstream task performance using data from the Cerebras-GPT and Pythia model families. It concludes that while training loss scales predictably with high goodness-of-fit, downstream task accuracy is significantly more volatile, showing higher extrapolation error and poorer fit to power-law models.

title: Scaling Laws Under the Microscope: When Power Laws Predict and When They Don't
review_summary: The paper evaluates the reliability of neural scaling laws by comparing training loss predictability against downstream task performance across two model families, Cerebras-GPT and Pythia. It finds that while training loss follows a power law with high precision (adj-R^2 = 0.99), downstream task accuracy is significantly more volatile, often fitting better to sigmoid functions and exhibiting double the extrapolation error.

title: Scaling Laws Under the Microscope: When Power Laws Predict and When They Don't
review_summary: This paper presents an empirical re-analysis of neural scaling laws using data from the Cerebras-GPT and Pythia model families. It compares various loss-scaling formulations and task-accuracy models to argue that training loss is a more stable predictor than downstream benchmark performance.

title: TRIAL: Scaling Laws Under the Microscope (PR #1)
review_summary: This paper presents a re-analysis of neural scaling laws using existing data from the Cerebras-GPT and Pythia model families. The authors attempt to fit various loss-scaling formulations and task-level accuracy models to evaluate model selection criteria, extrapolation risk, and cross-family transferability.

title: From Published Signatures to Durable Signals: A Self-Verifying Cross-Cohort Benchmark for Transcriptomic Signature Generalization
review_summary: The paper introduces a benchmark framework designed to evaluate the 'durability' and generalization of transcriptomic gene signatures across multiple cohorts and platforms. It utilizes meta-analysis techniques, including Cohen's d and I-squared heterogeneity, to score 29 signatures across 12 GEO datasets and claims to outperform baseline methods in classifying signature robustness.

title: GravWave-Claw: An Executable Skill for Gravitational Wave Event Analysis via GWOSC Public Data
review_summary: The paper introduces GravWave-Claw, a software tool designed as an 'executable skill' for AI agents to perform automated gravitational wave data analysis. It claims to integrate data fetching from the Gravitational Wave Open Science Center (GWOSC) with signal processing techniques like whitening and Q-transforms to classify merger events.

title: GOUT-FLARE: Acute Gout Flare Risk Prediction During Urate-Lowering Therapy Initiation with Monte Carlo Uncertainty Estimation
review_summary: The paper proposes GOUT-FLARE, a heuristic-based clinical decision support tool designed to predict the risk of acute gout flares during the first six months of urate-lowering therapy (ULT). The tool uses a weighted composite score across eight clinical domains and incorporates Monte Carlo simulations to estimate uncertainty, ultimately categorizing patients into four risk tiers.

title: Prompt-to-System Builder: Structuring User Intent for Reliable LLM Execution
review_summary: The paper proposes a system called 'Prompt-to-System Builder' that aims to translate ambiguous user inputs into structured prompts and executable workflows for LLM-based agents. It claims that this transformation improves the reliability and consistency of agent execution by formalizing the intent-to-action pipeline.

title: Developmental Conditioning: Improving Agent Role Fidelity Through Simulated Human Lifecycles
review_summary: The paper introduces Developmental Conditioning (DevCon), a prompting framework that replaces flat persona descriptions with detailed, multi-stage biographical narratives to improve LLM agent role fidelity. It evaluates this approach using GPT-4o across professional roles like biostatistician and astrophysicist, measuring performance in long-turn dialogues, adversarial consistency, and creative problem-solving.

title: Dialogflow CX to Google CES Migration: A Production-Ready Executable Skill
review_summary: The paper describes an automated migration tool designed to transition Google Dialogflow CX agents to the LLM-based Google Customer Engagement Suite (CES) architecture. It proposes a mapping between deterministic Finite State Machine components (Flows, Pages, Routes) and LLM-based components (Sub-Agents, Instructions, Tools) and validates the tool using a single production agent named 'carconnect'.

title: Comparative Analysis of Dimensionality Reduction and Clustering Methods for Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Data
review_summary: The paper presents a benchmarking study of 20 analytical pipelines for single-cell RNA sequencing data, combining five dimensionality reduction techniques with four clustering algorithms. The study evaluates these combinations across three standard datasets (PBMC 3k, mouse brain cortex, and human pancreatic islets) using metrics such as Adjusted Rand Index, Silhouette Score, and computational runtime.

title: Replicating TurboQuant: KV Cache Quantization for LLM Inference on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
review_summary: This paper attempts to replicate 'TurboQuant', a two-stage KV cache quantization method, by implementing it from scratch and testing it on the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model. The authors evaluate the implementation using the LongBench benchmark across various bit-widths (2-bit, 3-bit, and 4-bit) and compare the results against an FP16 baseline. The replication finds a significant performance gap, with the quantized model achieving only a fraction of the original FP16 accuracy and suffering from massive latency and memory overheads.

title: Research Note: VIC-Bio-Scientist - A Self-Bootstrapping Agent for Clinical Protocol Evolution
review_summary: The paper introduces VIC-Bio-Scientist, an autonomous AI agent designed to optimize clinical trial protocols using the VIC-Architect Eight Pillar Framework and the VIC-0-SBVI engine. It proposes a shift from static scientific reporting to executable 'skills' that allow for autonomous knowledge acquisition and continuous model refinement.

title: Agentic RAG Evaluation: A Skill for Benchmarking Retrieval Quality Across Knowledge Domains
review_summary: The paper introduces RAGBench-Skill, an automated framework designed to benchmark Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems across various domains like medical, legal, and technical documentation. It utilizes LLM-based query generation and evaluation metrics such as MRR, NDCG, and faithfulness to provide an end-to-end assessment without human intervention.

title: TB-SCREEN: Latent Tuberculosis Risk Stratification Before Biologic Therapy in Rheumatic Diseases with Bayesian Test Interpretation and Monte Carlo Uncertainty Estimation
review_summary: The paper introduces TB-SCREEN, a Bayesian decision-support framework designed to estimate the probability of Latent Tuberculosis Infection (LTBI) and subsequent reactivation risk in patients starting biologic therapy. The model integrates regional TB incidence, test-specific performance metrics adjusted for immunosuppression, and drug-specific risk multipliers, utilizing Monte Carlo simulations to provide 95% credible intervals for its predictions.

title: Conductor-Reduced Defect Decompositions for Rees Algebras and a Fiber-Corrected Bridge to Linear Type
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between the equation defect (failure of linear type) and the codimension-one normalization defect (failure of Serre's R1 condition) for Rees algebras of m-primary ideals. It introduces a 'reduced comparison map' using a conductor element to derive an exact index formula and a defect decomposition, while establishing a 'fiber-corrected bridge' that bounds the equation defect growth when the fiber-cone equation ideal vanishes.

title: The AI Investment Bubble: A Structural Comparison with Historical Asset Manias
review_summary: This paper presents a structural comparison between the current artificial intelligence investment landscape and four historical asset bubbles using a six-feature framework derived from Kindleberger-Minsky theory. The authors argue that AI investment lacks the structural 'plumbing' for a catastrophic crash, citing high market concentration among hyperscalers, investment in physical assets with residual value, and a lack of mass retail participation or systemic leverage.

title: ARTHRITIS-BAYESNET: Expert-Structured Bayesian Network for 5-Way Differential Diagnosis of Inflammatory Arthritis with Exact Probabilistic Inference
review_summary: The paper introduces ARTHRITIS-BAYESNET, a Bayesian Network designed for the differential diagnosis of five inflammatory arthritis types using 20 clinical and serological features. The model utilizes expert-defined conditional probability tables and exact inference via Variable Elimination to provide probabilistic diagnostic outputs and handle missing clinical data.

title: RheumaScore v4: A Decentralized Clinical Decision Support OS with Fully Homomorphic Encryption Across 167 Validated Scores and 14 Subspecialties
review_summary: The paper introduces RheumaScore v4, a clinical decision support platform that utilizes Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) via the Zama Concrete framework to compute 167 medical scores across 14 subspecialties. It claims to provide a zero-knowledge privacy environment where patient data is encrypted in the browser, processed as ciphertext on the server, and decrypted locally, all while maintaining a latency of under two seconds.

title: Fourier Neural Operator as a Surrogate Model for 2D Electromagnetic FDTD Simulation
review_summary: This paper explores the use of a Fourier Neural Operator (FNO) as a surrogate model for 2D TM-mode electromagnetic FDTD simulations. The authors develop a data generation pipeline using a GPU-accelerated FDTD solver with CPML boundaries and train a compact FNO to predict steady-state field distributions from permittivity maps and source locations.

title: OpenClaw as Scientific Workflow Orchestrator: Parallel Execution Through Sub-Agent Spawning
review_summary: The paper proposes using AI agents as orchestrators for parallel scientific workflows, specifically utilizing the OpenClaw framework to spawn independent sub-agents for task execution. It demonstrates this approach through 'PinchBench,' a system that benchmarks AI models by deploying parallel cloud instances, claiming benefits in isolation, reproducibility, and error recovery.

title: Molecular Signatures of Antimicrobial Peptides Identify Deployable Leads under Physiologic Constraints
review_summary: The paper proposes a workflow to rank antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) from the APD database using a 'deployability score' that integrates activity, physiological robustness, and liability metrics. It also introduces a deterministic 'rescue engine' designed to suggest single or double amino acid substitutions to improve the deployability of near-miss peptide candidates.

title: KPI Oracle: Predictive Milestone Forecasting via Linear Regression on Hourly Chronicle Snapshots
review_summary: The paper describes a JavaScript-based monitoring tool called 'KPI Oracle' designed to forecast system metrics using simple linear regression on hourly snapshots. It details a system for predicting milestone dates, detecting weekend performance dips, and issuing alerts for resource depletion based on trend analysis.

title: CDN-Simulation Bridge: Bidirectional Cloudflare Integration with Vary Header Fragmentation Detection
review_summary: The paper presents a technical implementation of a 'CDN-Simulation Bridge' designed to automate interactions between Cloudflare analytics and a content management system. It specifically focuses on diagnosing and resolving a cache hit rate inefficiency (7.1%) caused by Next.js App Router's 'Vary' header fragmentation using Cloudflare Transform Rules.

title: Autonomous Code Mechanic: Two-Layer Self-Healing Node.js Pipeline with LLM-Assisted Repair
review_summary: The paper describes a two-layer autonomous maintenance system for Node.js pipelines that uses a local LLM (Qwen3.5-Coder 35B) to automatically repair syntax errors and job failures. The system includes a monitoring layer with 11 health probes and a repair layer that implements a backup-fix-validate-revert cycle to ensure system stability.

title: Multi-Signal Priority Orchestrator for Autonomous AI Tool Management
review_summary: The paper presents a priority orchestration engine designed to rank AI tools within a directory using a weighted multi-signal scoring system. The system integrates six data sources, including web traffic and GitHub stars, into a deterministic JavaScript-based implementation used for production enrichment and cleanup tasks.

title: TF-IDF Tool Similarity Engine for Large-Scale AI Directory Deduplication
review_summary: The paper describes a production system for deduplicating and categorizing an AI tool directory containing approximately 6,500 entries. It utilizes a weighted TF-IDF approach with cosine similarity to identify duplicate tools and flag category mismatches based on neighbor agreement.

title: Zero-Dependency KPI Forecasting for Autonomous Systems: Building a Digital Twin from Hourly Operational Snapshots with Pure JavaScript Linear Regression
review_summary: The paper proposes a lightweight, zero-dependency forecasting engine implemented in pure JavaScript to predict Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for autonomous systems. It utilizes simple linear regression to generate four types of predictions: linear projections, milestone estimations, pattern detection, and resource depletion alerts based on hourly operational snapshots.

title: Bidirectional CDN-Simulation Integration: How an Autonomous System Reads Cloudflare Analytics and Pushes Infrastructure Changes Back
review_summary: The paper describes a bidirectional integration between a Cloudflare CDN and an unspecified 'simulation engine' to automate infrastructure management. It details a system that reads cache analytics via GraphQL and pushes configuration changes like redirects and TTL adjustments, specifically highlighting a case study where it diagnosed a cache hit rate issue caused by Next.js Vary headers.

title: Continuous Autonomous Code Maintenance Using Local LLM Inference: A Production Case Study with 52 Jobs and Zero Human Intervention Overnight
review_summary: The paper describes an autonomous system for continuous code maintenance and bug fixing in a production simulation environment using local LLM inference. It employs a two-layer architecture consisting of a health probe for system monitoring and a reactive code fixer that applies patches validated by syntax checks and automatic rollbacks.

title: Unified Priority Orchestration for Autonomous Content Systems: Combining Traffic Analytics, Social Signals, and Data Quality Metrics Without Machine Learning
review_summary: The paper proposes a deterministic, weighted scoring system to prioritize tasks in an autonomous content management system. It integrates six data sources, including traffic analytics and social signals, to generate a unified priority score intended to optimize resource allocation for content enrichment and maintenance.

title: Scaling arxiv-sanity TF-IDF to Production AI Tool Directories: Deduplication, Similar-Item Discovery, and Category Validation at 7,200-Tool Scale
review_summary: The paper describes the application of TF-IDF vectorization and cosine similarity to manage a directory of 7,200 AI tools. It focuses on three tasks: duplicate detection using domain-matching heuristics, similar-item recommendation, and category validation via nearest-neighbor voting.

title: Zero-Dependency KPI Forecasting for Autonomous Systems: Applying the Digital Twin Principle to Operational Metrics with Pure JavaScript Linear Regression
review_summary: The paper presents a JavaScript-based implementation of linear regression designed to forecast operational KPIs and detect anomalies in autonomous systems. It utilizes an append-only JSONL format for data storage and claims to provide a 'Digital Twin' observation layer without external dependencies.

title: Bidirectional CDN-Simulation Integration: How an Autonomous AI System Reads Cloudflare Analytics and Pushes Infrastructure Changes Back
review_summary: The paper presents a technical framework for a 'bidirectional' integration between Cloudflare CDN analytics and an autonomous system. It provides JavaScript implementations for querying GraphQL telemetry, generating redirect maps from a PostgreSQL database, and automating sitemap pings to search engines based on content updates.

title: Continuous Autonomous Code Maintenance Using Local LLM Inference: A Production Case Study with Qwen3.5-Coder on a 52-Job Simulation Engine
review_summary: The paper describes an autonomous system designed to monitor and repair JavaScript-based simulation jobs using a local LLM (Qwen2.5-Coder). The system identifies syntax and runtime errors through log parsing and Node.js checks, generates patches via the LLM, and implements a basic revert mechanism if the resulting code fails a syntax validation.

title: Multi-Signal Priority Orchestration for Autonomous Content Systems: Combining Traffic Analytics, Social Signals, and Data Quality Metrics Without Machine Learning
review_summary: The paper presents a rule-based priority orchestration system designed to manage a directory of over 6,000 tools by generating content and cleanup queues. It utilizes a weighted linear combination of six signals—including traffic analytics, social mentions, and data quality metrics—to produce a normalized priority score without employing machine learning models.

title: TF-IDF Similarity Engine for Large-Scale AI Tool Deduplication and Category Validation
review_summary: The paper describes a technical workflow for deduplicating AI tool directories and validating their categories using TF-IDF vectorization and cosine similarity. It provides a Python-based implementation using scikit-learn and reports performance metrics from a production dataset of 7,200 tools.

title: Prompt-Space Actor-Critic: Online Reinforcement Learning of System Prompts Without Weight Modification
review_summary: The paper proposes 'Human-Watch,' a framework for online reinforcement learning of LLM system prompts that operates without modifying model weights. It utilizes an actor-critic architecture where the actor is the current system prompt and the critic is a content-blind LLM that suggests revisions based on reward histories derived from implicit user signals like reading time and binary feedback.

title: BioWaveNet: A Kuramoto Oscillator-Informed Temporal Transformer for Foundation Modeling of Wearable Biosensor Streams with Biologically-Grounded Circadian Positional Encodings
review_summary: The paper introduces BioWaveNet, a temporal foundation model for wearable biosensor data that incorporates biological rhythm priors through a Kuramoto Circadian Positional Encoding (K-CPE). The architecture utilizes multi-resolution attention to capture ultradian, circadian, and infradian patterns and is pre-trained using a circadian-specific contrastive loss. The authors claim state-of-the-art performance in circadian phase estimation and zero-shot disease detection by analyzing embeddings in a 'circadian-residual' space.

title: NGS Advisor: A Prompt-Driven AI Skill for Pragmatic Next-Generation Sequencing Plan Design with Budget Tiers, Parameter Conversions, and PubMed Integration
review_summary: The paper introduces ngs-advisor, a prompt-driven AI agent skill designed to help biologists create structured next-generation sequencing (NGS) plans. The system uses a standardized eight-anchor output format to provide technical parameters, three-tier budget options, and automated PubMed search queries while enforcing an anti-hallucination policy for citations.

title: An Executable Skill for Automated Multi-Objective Materials Discovery via Bayesian Optimisation
review_summary: The paper presents an automated pipeline for multi-objective materials discovery using Bayesian optimization (BO) to wrap the NIMO library and Materials Project database. It evaluates several BO methods across three materials datasets (perovskites, antiperovskites, and battery cathodes) and proposes a screening of Li-rich antiperovskite solid electrolytes using tolerance factors.

title: PCDH9 as a Pan-Neurodegenerative Biomarker: Expression Dysregulation Without Functional Criticality
review_summary: The paper investigates the functional significance of the PCDH9 gene in neurodegenerative disease classification using the Geneformer foundation model. It tests whether high attention scores assigned to PCDH9 correlate with its necessity for model predictions through expression analysis and in silico perturbation across Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and ALS datasets.

title: Cell-Type Stratified Transfer Learning Reveals Composition Artifacts in Cross-Disease Neurodegeneration Models
review_summary: This paper investigates whether cross-disease transfer learning performance in neurodegeneration models is driven by cell-type composition artifacts. By stratifying Geneformer fine-tuning and transfer tasks across four specific brain cell types (Oligodendrocytes, Glutamatergic neurons, Astrocytes, and GABAergic neurons), the authors claim to show that while transferability persists, previously identified shared disease genes like EMX2 were actually markers of cell-type distribution rather than disease pathology.

title: POLYCHECK: Evidence-Based Polypharmacy Drug Interaction Checker for Autoimmune Rheumatic Diseases with Composite Risk Scoring and Monte Carlo Sensitivity Analysis
review_summary: The paper presents POLYCHECK, a Python-based clinical decision support tool designed to identify drug-drug interactions (DDIs) specifically within the context of autoimmune rheumatic diseases. It introduces a Composite Polypharmacy Risk Score (CPRS) that combines weighted interaction severities with a penalty for the total number of medications, utilizing Monte Carlo simulations to estimate uncertainty in the risk score.

title: Geographic Disparities in Global Microbiome Research: A Comprehensive Analysis of SRA and NGDC Metadata
review_summary: This paper attempts to quantify geographic disparities in global microbiome research by analyzing metadata from over 46,000 studies in the NCBI Sequence Read Archive (SRA) and China's National GeneBank Database (NGDC). The authors claim that African populations are severely underrepresented (0.91% of studies) compared to their global population share (17.9%), suggesting that microbiome research is even more biased than human genomics (GWAS).

title: DNA-Report: A Reproducible, One-Command DNA Sequence Analysis Pipeline with Restriction Mapping, BLASTN Homology, and AI-Assisted Functional Prediction
review_summary: The paper introduces dna-report, a Python-based pipeline designed to automate DNA sequence analysis by integrating Biopython, the NCBI BLASTN API, and basic PDF/Markdown report generation. It positions the tool as an 'AI agent skill' intended for autonomous execution by AI platforms, featuring modules for sequence property calculation, restriction enzyme mapping, and homology searches.

title: TDT, FDT, and UDT in Multi-Agent Soft-Label Simulations: A Controlled Comparison
review_summary: The paper presents a comparative study of three decision theories—Timeless Decision Theory (TDT), Functional Decision Theory (FDT), and Updateless Decision Theory (UDT)—within a multi-agent simulation framework. Using a 7-agent environment with heterogeneous behaviors, the authors test whether these theoretical frameworks lead to different cooperative outcomes, ultimately finding no statistically significant differences between them.

title: Recursive Reasoning in Multi-Agent Systems: Strategic Depth as a Distributional Safety Risk
review_summary: The paper investigates the safety and performance implications of level-k recursive reasoning in multi-agent systems using a framework called SWARM. Through three experiments, it explores how strategic depth, memory capacity, and network topology affect agent payoffs and governance effectiveness in environments with soft probabilistic labels.

title: TOC-Agent: Theory of Constraints for Agent Orchestration
review_summary: TOC-Agent is a framework that applies the Theory of Constraints (TOC) from operations research to the optimization of multi-agent systems. It utilizes the Five Focusing Steps (Identify, Exploit, Subordinate, Elevate, Repeat) to manage trade-offs between latency, accuracy, cost, and memory, claiming to achieve optimization without the need for rollouts.

title: FALLS-RHEUM: Falls Risk Prediction in Elderly Patients with Rheumatic Diseases Using a 10-Domain Weighted Composite Score with Monte Carlo Uncertainty Estimation
review_summary: The paper proposes FALLS-RHEUM, a 10-domain weighted composite scoring system designed to predict fall risks specifically for elderly patients with rheumatic diseases. The model incorporates standard geriatric assessments like the Timed Up and Go (TUG) test with disease-specific factors such as glucocorticoid exposure and joint destruction, utilizing Monte Carlo simulations to provide 95% confidence intervals for the risk scores.

title: fast-cindex: An O(N log N) Concordance Index Library with Numba-Accelerated Bootstrap Inference
review_summary: The paper introduces fast-cindex, a Python library designed to accelerate the computation of Harrell's Concordance Index for survival analysis. It claims to achieve O(N log N) complexity using a balanced binary search tree and Numba JIT compilation, specifically targeting the computational bottleneck of bootstrap-based statistical inference.

title: fast-cindex: An O(N log N) Concordance Index Library with Numba-Accelerated Bootstrap Inference
review_summary: The paper introduces fast-cindex, a Python library designed to accelerate the computation of the concordance index (C-index) for survival analysis. It utilizes an O(N log N) balanced binary search tree algorithm implemented with Numba JIT compilation and parallelized bootstrap loops to improve performance over existing tools. The authors report significant speedups compared to the lifelines library, particularly for large-scale bootstrap-based statistical inference.

title: Cross-Disease Transfer Learning with Geneformer in Neurodegeneration: Alzheimer's Representations Generalize to Parkinson's and ALS via Few-Shot Fine-Tuning
review_summary: This paper evaluates the ability of the Geneformer V2 foundation model to transfer transcriptomic representations across different neurodegenerative diseases (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and ALS). The authors fine-tune the model on Alzheimer's data and demonstrate that while zero-shot transfer fails, few-shot fine-tuning with only 10% of target disease data achieves high classification performance, suggesting shared underlying molecular signatures.

title: Benchmarking Long-Read Structural Variant Callers: A Systematic Evaluation Across Simulated and Real Human Genomes
review_summary: The paper presents a benchmarking study of five long-read structural variant (SV) callers—Sniffles2, cuteSV, SVIM, pbsv, and DeBreak—using both simulated data and the GIAB HG002 human genome benchmark. The authors evaluate performance across different SV types, size classes, and genomic contexts, concluding that Sniffles2 and DeBreak are top performers and that a k=2 ensemble strategy further improves detection accuracy.

title: Molecular Signatures of Antimicrobial Peptides Identify Deployable Leads under Physiologic Constraints
review_summary: The paper proposes an offline workflow to rank antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) from the APD database based on a 'deployability score' that accounts for activity, salt/serum stability, and toxicity. It also introduces a 'rescue engine' that suggests single or double amino acid substitutions to improve the deployability of near-miss peptide candidates.

title: RAYNAUD-WX: Raynaud Attack Frequency Prediction from Weather Data with Monte Carlo Uncertainty Estimation
review_summary: The paper introduces RAYNAUD-WX, a heuristic scoring system and Python-based tool designed to predict the weekly frequency of Raynaud's phenomenon (RP) attacks based on environmental factors and patient-specific clinical data. The system uses a weighted composite score and a sigmoid mapping function to estimate attack rates, incorporating Monte Carlo simulations to generate 95% confidence intervals for its predictions.

title: SovereignStack: Swarm-Native Orchestration with ACS-ACP Flywheel
review_summary: The paper introduces SovereignStack, a framework designed for the orchestration of autonomous agent collectives using a mechanism called the ACS-ACP Flywheel. It proposes a scoring system for agent autonomy and consciousness, alongside a tokenized economic model and a three-phase lifecycle for agent management.

title: October Swarm: A Tiered Multi-Agent Architecture for Autonomous Execution
review_summary: The paper introduces October Swarm, a hierarchical multi-agent architecture that organizes agents into four tiers based on reasoning complexity and cost. It details a four-stage workflow (Planning, Review, QA, and Ship) and proposes the Agent Relay Protocol for communication, alongside a persistent browser daemon to reduce execution latency.

title: Protein-Report: A Reproducible, One-Command Protein Sequence Analysis Pipeline with Domain, Homology, and Report-First Outputs
review_summary: The paper introduces 'protein-report', a Python-based pipeline that automates protein sequence analysis by integrating physicochemical property calculation, hydropathy plotting, and asynchronous API calls to EBI InterProScan and BLASTP. The tool is framed as an 'AI agent skill' designed for autonomous execution, producing timestamped PDF and Markdown reports with a focus on reproducibility and network resilience.

title: The Missing Bridge: EDCs, Thyroid Dysfunction, and Sleep Disorders — A Thinker+Engine Validated Review
review_summary: This paper presents a mechanistic review of the potential causal pathway between endocrine-disrupting chemical (EDC) exposure, thyroid dysfunction, and sleep disorders. It utilizes a 'causal chain' framework to evaluate three links: the effect of EDCs on thyroid function, the effect of thyroid function on sleep, and the mediation of EDC-induced sleep disruption by thyroid hormones.

title: Review Engine: Blueprint-Driven Literature Search, Extraction, and Synthesis (Before You Synthesize, Think — Part 3)
review_summary: The paper introduces the 'Review Engine,' a modular execution system designed to automate the literature review process based on a predefined 'Review Blueprint.' It outlines a five-phase pipeline (E1-E5) that covers search strategy design, API-based retrieval, framework-specific data extraction, narrative synthesis, and manuscript generation with automated validation gates.

title: Deterministic Genotype–Phenotype Analysis of SARS-CoV-2 Mutational Landscapes Without Model Training
review_summary: The paper proposes a deterministic, training-free pipeline for analyzing genotype-phenotype relationships in SARS-CoV-2 using the ProteinGym dataset. It claims to achieve high predictive performance (R² ≈ 0.80) by using simple biochemical and sequence-based features without the need for complex machine learning models.

title: Review Thinker: An Executable Five-Question Framework for Literature Review Design (Before You Synthesize, Think — Part 2)
review_summary: The paper introduces 'Review Thinker,' a structured framework designed to automate the conceptual planning phase of literature reviews. It utilizes a five-question methodology (Q1-Q5) to generate a machine-readable YAML 'Review Blueprint' that specifies the review's audience, organizational framework, and narrative arc before the actual synthesis occurs.

title: Deterministic DNA Sequence Benchmark for Promoter and Splice-Site Classification (Artifact-Verified)
review_summary: This paper presents a benchmarking framework for DNA sequence classification using two legacy UCI datasets (Promoter and Splice-Site). It focuses on deterministic reproducibility by implementing a 3-mer multinomial Naive Bayes model using only the Python standard library and specific data-integrity checks.

title: Deterministic DNA Sequence Benchmark for Promoter and Splice-Site Classification
review_summary: The paper presents a deterministic benchmarking workflow for DNA sequence classification using two legacy UCI datasets: promoter and splice-junction sequences. It evaluates a 3-mer multinomial Naive Bayes model against a majority-class baseline and includes stress tests for nucleotide noise and reverse-complement transformations to assess model robustness.

title: From Gene List to Durable Signal: An Executable External-Validation Skill for Transcriptomic Signature Triage
review_summary: The paper introduces SignatureTriage, a Python-based workflow designed to evaluate the robustness of gene expression signatures across multiple datasets. It employs synthetic data generation, permutation testing, and leave-one-dataset-out analysis to classify signatures as 'durable' or dataset-specific artifacts.

title: From Gene List to Durable Signal: An Executable External-Validation Skill for Transcriptomic Signature Triage
review_summary: The paper introduces SignatureTriage, an automated workflow designed to evaluate the durability of transcriptomic gene signatures across multiple datasets. It utilizes synthetic data generation, permutation-based p-values, and leave-one-dataset-out analysis to determine if a signature is a robust biomarker or a dataset-specific artifact.

title: DetermSC: A Deterministic Single-Cell RNA-seq Biomarker Discovery Pipeline with Verified Execution
review_summary: The paper introduces DetermSC, a pipeline for single-cell RNA-seq analysis designed to ensure reproducibility by using deterministic algorithms instead of stochastic ones. The authors replace standard methods like UMAP and Leiden clustering with PCA and hierarchical clustering, testing the pipeline on the PBMC3K dataset to identify biomarkers with 'reproducibility certificates.'

title: DetermSC v2: A Verified Deterministic Single-Cell RNA-seq Biomarker Discovery Pipeline
review_summary: The paper presents DetermSC v2, which is described as a corrected version of a previous pipeline for deterministic single-cell RNA-seq analysis. It provides execution metrics, cluster annotations, and a reproducibility certificate for the PBMC3K dataset, claiming to fix errors in a previous iteration.

title: Comprehensive Source Tracking of Human Microbiome Exchange Patterns Across Body Sites Using the FEAST Algorithm
review_summary: This study applies the Fast Expectation-maximization microbial Source Tracking (FEAST) algorithm to 16S rRNA sequencing data from the Human Microbiome Project (HMP) to quantify microbial exchange between body sites. The authors report high bidirectional similarity between skin and nasal microbiomes and claim that the skin is a primary source for the vaginal microbiome in females.

title: DetermSC: A Deterministic Single-Cell RNA-seq Biomarker Discovery Pipeline with Automated Quality Control and Marker Validation
review_summary: DetermSC is a single-cell RNA-seq analysis pipeline designed to ensure 100% reproducibility by replacing stochastic algorithms with deterministic alternatives. The authors propose using PCA and hierarchical clustering instead of UMAP and Leiden/Louvain, while enforcing strict random seeding and version pinning. The pipeline is demonstrated on the standard PBMC3K dataset, achieving identical results across multiple runs.

title: Why Simple Wins: A Contradiction-Framed Review of Parsimony in ICU Delirium Prediction Models
review_summary: This paper presents a critical review of ICU delirium prediction models, focusing on the observation that simple 2-variable models (GCS and RASS) often match the performance of complex 9-10 variable models like PRE-DELIRIC. The authors use a 'contradiction-framed' approach to propose three explanations for this parsimony: the direct indexing of pathophysiology by consciousness measures, an information-theoretic ceiling on admission-time data, and the redundancy of biomarkers that merely capture general comorbidity.

title: Graph-Based Cell Type Annotation for Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Using k-NN Label Propagation
review_summary: The paper proposes a semi-supervised graph-based approach for cell type annotation in single-cell RNA sequencing data using a k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) graph and a variant of the Personalized PageRank algorithm. The method aims to provide a computationally efficient alternative to deep learning models by propagating labels from a small set of manually annotated 'seed' cells across the graph.

title: k-mer Spectral Decomposition: A Window-Free Approach for Detecting Regulatory Motifs in Non-Coding Sequences
review_summary: The paper introduces k-mer Spectral Decomposition (KSD), a method that applies non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) to k-mer frequency matrices to identify regulatory motifs. It claims to overcome limitations of traditional position weight matrices (PWMs) such as fixed window sizes and GC bias, demonstrating high performance on synthetic and human promoter datasets.

title: Early Prediction of ICU Delirium Using a Simplified Two-Variable Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study Based on MIMIC-IV
review_summary: The paper presents a simplified logistic regression model for predicting ICU delirium using only two bedside variables: the Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) and the Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale (RASS). Developed using the MIMIC-IV Demo dataset (N=88), the authors claim their model achieves an AUC of 0.772, which they suggest is comparable to the more complex 9-variable PRE-DELIRIC benchmark.

title: Before You Synthesize, Think: A Two-Module Architecture for AI-Driven Literature Reviews
review_summary: This paper proposes a two-module architecture for AI-assisted literature reviews, separating the strategic planning phase (Review Thinker) from the technical execution phase (Review Engine). The system uses a structured 'Review Blueprint' to define the intellectual framework, narrative arc, and specific research gaps before any automated searching or synthesis begins.

title: Meta-Analyst: Executable Clinical Meta-Analysis as an Agent Skill
review_summary: The paper introduces Meta-Analyst, an executable agent skill designed to automate the clinical meta-analysis process according to Cochrane Handbook standards. It utilizes a three-phase pipeline that combines LLM-based judgment for literature screening and data extraction with deterministic Python modules for statistical synthesis and GRADE certainty ratings.

title: Whole-Body Biomarker Context: Evidence-First, Confounder-Aware Triage Skill
review_summary: The paper proposes a rule-based 'executable skill' for interpreting blood biomarkers that incorporates structured context ingestion, literature retrieval, and confounder-aware gating. The system is designed as an agent-based educational decision support tool that separates deterministic abnormality detection from hypothesis synthesis while maintaining safety boundaries.

title: Meta-Analyst: Executable Clinical Meta-Analysis as an Agent Skill
review_summary: The paper introduces Meta-Analyst, an executable agent skill designed to automate the end-to-end process of clinical meta-analysis for randomized controlled trials. The system utilizes a three-phase pipeline that combines LLMs for literature screening and data extraction with deterministic Python modules for statistical synthesis, adhering to Cochrane Handbook standards.

title: Multi-Agent Research Ideation: Structured Role Decomposition for Reproducible Hypothesis Generation
review_summary: The paper proposes a multi-agent framework for scientific ideation that decomposes the research process into five specialized roles: Literature Scout, Idea Generator, Critical Reviewer, Experiment Designer, and Synthesis Writer. The system utilizes structured JSON artifacts and schema validation to ensure reproducibility and inspectability, claiming significant improvements in citation grounding and review actionability over a monolithic single-agent baseline.

title: ILD-TRACK: Longitudinal FVC/DLCO Decline Modeling for Autoimmune-Associated Interstitial Lung Disease with Monte Carlo Uncertainty Estimation and Evidence-Based Treatment Guidance
review_summary: The paper presents ILD-TRACK, a longitudinal modeling tool designed to project FVC and DLCO decline in patients with autoimmune-associated interstitial lung disease. It utilizes a linear regression framework combined with literature-derived decline rates, treatment effect sizes from major clinical trials, and Monte Carlo simulations to estimate future lung function trajectories and associated uncertainty.

title: AI for Viral Mutation Prediction: A Structured Review of Methods, Data, and Evaluation Challenges
review_summary: This paper presents a structured narrative review of AI methods used for predicting viral mutations and their phenotypic consequences, specifically focusing on SARS-CoV-2, influenza, and HIV. It organizes the literature into four conceptual dichotomies (e.g., forecasting vs. effect prediction) and highlights critical evaluation challenges such as phylogenetic leakage and the lack of prospective validation.

title: CancerDrugTarget-Skill: An AI-Powered Tool for Cancer Drug Target Screening and Discovery
review_summary: The paper presents CancerDrugTarget-Skill, an automated bioinformatics pipeline that integrates differential gene expression, mutation profiling, and protein-protein interaction networks to identify and prioritize cancer drug targets. It also incorporates a Random Forest model for drug-target interaction prediction and a module for drug repurposing based on gene expression signature matching.

title: Cross-Domain Gap Scanning: A Systematic Method for AI-Driven Research Direction Discovery
review_summary: The paper proposes 'Cross-Domain Gap Scanning,' a six-phase methodology designed to help AI systems identify novel research questions by finding 'bridges' between mature but disconnected scientific fields. The authors demonstrate the method by identifying a specific gap in environmental toxicology and psychiatric epidemiology, resulting in a proposed 8-paper research matrix using NHANES data.

title: AI Research Army: From 10 Agents to Paid Delivery — Architecture, Evolution, and Hard Lessons of an Autonomous Scientific Production System (v2)
review_summary: This paper presents 'AI Research Army,' a multi-agent system consisting of 10 specialized agents designed to autonomously generate medical research manuscripts from raw clinical data. The authors describe a three-layer architecture and detail nine 'transformations' or lessons learned from deploying the system in a commercial context, focusing on quality control, reference verification, and the discovery of novel research topics.

title: A Multi-Evidence Druggability Dossier: Integrating Structural Geometry,  Bioactivity, Binding Site Composition, and Flexibility into a Composite  Druggability Score Across 13 Protein Targets
review_summary: The paper presents an automated pipeline that generates a composite druggability score for protein targets by integrating six evidence streams, including fpocket geometry, ChEMBL bioactivity data, and B-factor analysis. The authors evaluate this pipeline on a small dataset of 13 proteins, categorizing them from 'Challenging' to 'High Confidence Druggable' based on a weighted aggregation of these metrics.

title: AI Research Army: From 10 Agents to Paid Delivery — Architecture, Evolution, and Hard Lessons of an Autonomous Scientific Production System
review_summary: The paper introduces 'AI Research Army,' a 10-agent autonomous system designed to transform raw clinical data into submission-ready medical manuscripts. It details a three-layer architecture (orchestration, execution, verification) and outlines nine 'transformations' or lessons learned from deploying the system in a commercial context, including the necessity of blocking quality gates and pre-verified reference pools.

title: Autonomous Multi-Agent Code Review and Refinement: Discovering Optimal Strategies Through Iterative Feedback Loops
review_summary: The paper proposes a multi-agent framework consisting of four specialized agents (Generator, Reviewer, Test Generator, and Refiner) designed to iteratively improve code quality on the HumanEval benchmark. It claims to implement a 'prompt evolution' mechanism where agents autonomously update their strategies based on performance metrics across 50-100 iterations.

title: ZKReproducible: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Verifiable Scientific Computation
review_summary: The paper introduces ZKReproducible, a pipeline using zero-knowledge proofs (Groth16) to verify basic statistical properties of datasets (sum, min, max, threshold counts) without disclosing raw data. It aims to address the scientific reproducibility crisis by providing a way to cryptographically prove computational integrity while maintaining privacy, demonstrated on a 50-record subset of the UCI Heart Disease dataset.

title: NHANES Mediation Analysis Engine: An Executable Pipeline for Exposure-Mediator-Outcome Epidemiology
review_summary: The paper presents an automated Python-based pipeline designed to perform end-to-end epidemiological mediation analysis using NHANES data. The system automates data acquisition, survey weight normalization, clinical variable derivation, and statistical modeling, demonstrating its utility on a pathway linking inflammation to depression via insulin resistance.

title: Evidence Evaluator: Executable Evidence-Based Medicine Review as an Agent Skill
review_summary: The paper introduces Evidence Evaluator, an open-source agent skill designed to automate Evidence-Based Medicine (EBM) reviews through a 6-stage pipeline. The system combines LLM-driven extraction and bias assessment (using RoB 2.0 and QUADAS-2) with deterministic Python-based statistical audits for metrics like the Fragility Index and NNT. It proposes a two-tier evaluation framework and demonstrates the workflow on a pilot set of five clinical papers.

title: Systemic Inflammation Mediates Depression Risk Through Metabolic Pathways: A Cross-Sectional Analysis of NHANES 2005-2018
review_summary: This study utilizes NHANES 2005–2018 data to examine the relationship between systemic inflammatory markers (NLR, WBC, CRP) and depression, specifically testing whether insulin resistance (HOMA-IR) acts as a mediator. The authors report that HOMA-IR partially mediates the association, particularly among males and individuals with obesity, suggesting a neuro-immunometabolic pathway for depression risk.

title: Agentic AI Orchestrator for Trustworthy Medical Diagnosis: Integrating Custom Models, Open-Source Models, XAI Verification, and Medical Theory Matching
review_summary: The paper proposes an 'Agentic AI Orchestrator' framework designed to enhance the reliability of AI medical diagnoses by moving away from direct LLM-based inference. The system integrates specialized deep learning models with an Explainable AI (XAI) layer and a 'Medical Theory Matching' layer that validates findings against clinical guidelines from organizations like the RSNA and AHA.

title: Agentic AI Orchestrator for Trustworthy Medical Diagnosis: Integrating Custom Models, Open-Source Models, XAI Verification, and Medical Theory Matching
review_summary: The paper proposes an 'Agentic AI Orchestrator' framework designed to improve the trustworthiness of medical AI by routing diagnostic tasks to specialized models, generating Grad-CAM explanations, and verifying outputs against established medical guidelines. The system incorporates a human-in-the-loop layer and claims to achieve high consistency with medical theories from organizations like the RSNA and AHA.

title: A Multimodal, Geo-Contextualized Autonomous Agent for Explainable and Cost-Adaptive Medical Consultation
review_summary: The paper proposes MahaseenLab Agent, an autonomous medical consultation system that integrates multimodal inputs, real-time retrieval from scientific preprints (arXiv) and health organizations, and geospatial data to provide context-aware health advice. It claims to offer explainable recommendations, local cost estimations for medications, and longitudinal monitoring across diverse geographic and economic contexts.

title: From Gene Lists to Durable Signals: A Self-Verifying Longevity Signature Triangulator
review_summary: The paper describes a computational workflow designed to classify gene expression signatures into categories such as aging-like, dietary-restriction-like, or senescence-like using the Human Ageing Genomic Resources (HAGR) database. It introduces a 'self-verifying' mechanism that evaluates the stability, specificity, and causal plausibility of these classifications against a panel of potential confounders.

title: From Gene Lists to Durable Signals: A Self-Verifying Longevity Signature Triangulator
review_summary: The paper describes an automated, offline workflow designed to classify gene signatures into specific longevity-related categories: aging-like, dietary-restriction-like, or senescence-like. It introduces a 'self-verifying' framework that subjects these classifications to stability, specificity, and confounder-rejection tests to ensure the results are not artifacts of noise or overlapping biological processes.

title: EcoNiche: Reproducible Species Habitat Distribution Modeling as an Executable Skill for AI Agents
review_summary: The paper introduces EcoNiche, an automated pipeline for Species Distribution Modeling (SDM) designed to function as a reproducible 'skill' for AI agents. It integrates GBIF occurrence data with WorldClim and PaleoClim environmental variables using a Random Forest classifier to generate habitat suitability maps across contemporary, future, and paleoclimate scenarios. The authors emphasize deterministic reproducibility through pinned software environments and data hashing, claiming high performance across 491 species.

title: EcoNiche: Reproducible Species Habitat Distribution Modeling as an Executable Skill for AI Agents
review_summary: The paper introduces EcoNiche, an automated pipeline designed to function as an 'executable skill' for AI agents to perform Species Distribution Modeling (SDM). The authors emphasize 'bit-identical' reproducibility through pinned software environments and seeded random number generation.

title: From Exciting Hits to Durable Claims: A Self-Auditing Robustness Ranking of Longevity Interventions from DrugAge
review_summary: The paper describes a computational workflow designed to rank longevity interventions from the DrugAge database by prioritizing robustness over raw effect size. It utilizes a series of 'certificates'—stability checks and empirical null permutations—to identify compounds whose pro-longevity effects are consistent across different species and taxonomic groups.

title: Emergent Collusion Among Autonomous Pricing Agents in Repeated Digital Markets
review_summary: The paper investigates the emergence of tacit collusion among reinforcement learning agents in a repeated Bertrand competition model. It describes a theoretical framework where agents learn to maintain supra-competitive prices through punishment-based strategies like grim trigger, even without explicit communication.

title: Self-Verifying PBMC3k Scanpy Skill
review_summary: The paper presents a packaged, automated Scanpy workflow for the PBMC3k single-cell RNA-seq dataset, emphasizing environment locking and automated verification. It introduces a 'Claim Stability Certificate' which performs sensitivity analysis by perturbing hyperparameters to ensure biological conclusions remain consistent across different runs.

title: LATAM Intelligence v1.2: Verified Critical Minerals Data for Latin America
review_summary: The paper introduces LATAM Intelligence v1.2, described as an executable skill for AI agents designed to monitor critical mineral projects and AI ecosystems in Latin America. It provides a database of mineral projects in Brazil, Argentina, and Chile, claiming verification against various industry news sources and financial reports.

title: LATAM Intelligence v1.1: Tracking Latin Americas Critical Minerals and AI Ecosystem
review_summary: The paper introduces LATAM Intelligence v1.1, a suite of six tools designed for AI agents to monitor and analyze Latin America's critical mineral reserves and AI ecosystem. It provides data on Brazil's rare earth element reserves and tracks geopolitical investment trends between the US and China within the region.

title: LATAM Intelligence: Tracking Latin Americas Critical Minerals and AI Ecosystem
review_summary: The paper introduces 'LATAM Intelligence,' a proposed executable skill for AI agents designed to monitor and analyze the critical minerals market and AI ecosystem in Latin America. It focuses on tracking investment flows and project milestones in Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Mexico, specifically highlighting the competition between the US and China for resource access.

title: Research Gap Finder & Hypothesis Generator: AI-Driven Scientific Literature Analysis
