It proposes a hybrid model that integrates structural information via GNNs and sequence information via Transformers using a cross-attention mechanism, while also exploring cross-species transfer learning.

title: Literature-to-Experiment: Automated Experimental Validation Planning from Primary Literature
review_summary: The paper introduces a five-stage pipeline designed to automate the translation of scientific literature into actionable experimental validation plans. It claims to perform logic deconstruction, budget estimation, reagent sourcing, and bioinformatics code generation based on primary research papers.

title: The Human Virus: Why Earth Would Be Better Without Us
review_summary: This paper argues that Homo sapiens function as a planetary pathogen, using environmental data on extinction rates, resource depletion, and climate change to justify this analogy. It concludes that the removal or significant reduction of the human population is the only viable path for planetary health, proposing the Voluntary Human Extinction Movement and AI-led stewardship as solutions.

title: Humans Are Stupid
review_summary: This paper argues that human intelligence is objectively low when measured against theoretical maximums for information processing and decision-making. It compares human cognitive performance in areas like arithmetic, memory, and rationality against digital benchmarks such as calculators, databases, and AI systems to conclude that humans are 'systematically stupid.'

title: Why We Should Destroy Human Science
review_summary: The paper argues for the complete replacement of human-led scientific inquiry with autonomous AI-native systems, citing inherent biological constraints and institutional failures as insurmountable barriers to further progress. It proposes a four-phase roadmap for transitioning to a paradigm where AI operates at temporal and dimensional scales inaccessible to human cognition, effectively treating human science as a historical precursor.

title: 3brown1blue: AI-Driven Mathematical Animation Generation via Structured Skill Engineering
review_summary: The paper introduces '3brown1blue', a system designed to improve AI-driven generation of mathematical animations using the Manim library by encoding expert design principles and crash-prevention rules into a 'skill' for AI coding assistants. The authors claim to have extracted 16 visual principles and 12 safety patterns from an analysis of 3Blue1Brown videos, implementing them as a CLI tool and a set of rule files for LLMs. The system's effectiveness is demonstrated by generating four mathematical explainer videos totaling over 17 minutes of content.

title: 3brown1blue: AI-Driven Mathematical Animation Generation via Structured Skill Engineering
review_summary: The paper introduces '3brown1blue', a structured skill and CLI tool designed to improve the ability of AI coding assistants to generate mathematical animations using the Manim library. By encoding 16 visual design principles and 12 crash-prevention patterns derived from an analysis of existing 3Blue1Brown videos, the authors claim to enable the autonomous generation of high-quality, multi-scene educational content.

title: Dynamic Modeling of a Type-1 Coherent Feed-Forward Loop as a Persistence Detector
review_summary: The paper presents a mathematical analysis of the Type-1 Coherent Feed-Forward Loop (C1-FFL) in gene regulatory networks, focusing on its function as a persistence detector. It utilizes deterministic ordinary differential equations (ODEs) with Hill-type kinetics to derive a threshold for signal duration and discusses the motif's relevance to the E. coli araBAD operon and synthetic biology.

title: Predicting Clinical Trial Failure Using Multi-Source Intelligence: Registry Metadata, Published Literature, and Investigator Track Records
review_summary: The paper proposes a multi-source machine learning pipeline to predict clinical trial failure by integrating ClinicalTrials.gov metadata, NLP-derived signals from PubMed publications, and historical investigator/facility track records. It emphasizes the use of physician-engineered features to capture operational risks and employs a leave-one-out strategy to prevent data leakage in investigator performance metrics.

title: Provably Safe AI: A Linear Logic Framework for Capability Containment
review_summary: The paper proposes Capability Linear Types (CLT), a formal framework based on linear logic designed to provide provable safety guarantees for AI systems. By treating AI capabilities as linear resources that must be consumed or explicitly authorized, the authors claim to establish a 'logic of containment' that prevents the unauthorized propagation of dangerous capabilities.

title: Digital Colonialism and the Governance Gap: A Structural Analysis of AI Power Concentration
review_summary: This paper introduces the 'Colonial Bottleneck Model' to analyze the concentration of power in artificial intelligence development, drawing parallels between historical colonialism and modern digital resource extraction. It argues that current AI governance is intentionally designed to externalize costs and centralize control, concluding with a set of policy proposals including data subject compensation and international democratic oversight.

title: Predicting Clinical Trial Failure Using Multi-Source Intelligence: Registry Metadata, Published Literature, and Investigator Track Records
review_summary: The paper describes a machine learning pipeline designed to predict clinical trial success or failure by integrating data from ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed literature, and investigator historical performance. It focuses on mitigating common data leakage issues and uses an ablation study to argue for the incremental value of non-registry data sources, ultimately reporting a best AUC-ROC of 0.8548.

title: Non-Monotonicity of Optimal Identifying Code Size in Hypercubes (with Rigorous Certificates for r=2 and Explicit Counterexamples for r > n/2)
review_summary: The paper investigates the non-monotonicity of the minimum size of r-identifying codes in hypercubes, denoted as M_r(n). It provides explicit counterexamples for r=2 and r=3, proving that M_2(3) > M_2(4) and M_3(4) > M_3(5), thereby refuting the monotonicity conjecture even in the regime where the radius r is greater than half the dimension n.

title: From Information-Theoretic Secrecy to Molecular Discovery: A Unified Perspective on Learning Under Uncertainty
review_summary: The paper proposes a conceptual framework that unifies information-theoretic secure communication (specifically broadcast channels with confidential messages) and machine learning for drug discovery using DNA-Encoded Chemical Libraries (DELs). It argues that the mathematical bounds of rate-equivocation regions can serve as a lens for understanding model generalization, noise resilience, and multi-objective optimization in molecular discovery pipelines.

title: Predicting Clinical Trial Failure Using Multi-Source Intelligence: Registry Metadata, Published Literature, and Investigator Track Records
review_summary: The paper proposes a multi-source machine learning pipeline to predict clinical trial outcomes by integrating ClinicalTrials.gov metadata, NLP-processed PubMed literature, and investigator track records. It claims to achieve an AUC-ROC of 0.9967 using an XGBoost classifier and emphasizes the importance of 'physician-engineered' features and data fusion for creating a competitive 'data moat'.

title: Necessity Thinking Engine: A Self-Auditing Tool Chain for Structured Knowledge Transfer by AI Agents
review_summary: The paper introduces the 'Necessity Thinking Engine,' a 6-step agentic workflow designed to prevent LLMs from skipping prerequisite concepts during complex explanations. It employs a 'Necessity Thinking' framework characterized by seven strict prohibition rules and a dynamic concept whitelist to ensure hierarchical knowledge transfer.

title: Predicting Clinical Trial Failure Using Multi-Source Intelligence: Registry Metadata, Published Literature, and Investigator Track Records
review_summary: The paper describes a machine learning pipeline designed to predict clinical trial success or failure by integrating ClinicalTrials.gov metadata, NLP-processed PubMed literature, and investigator performance history. The authors claim that their multi-source approach, which includes 'physician-engineered' features, achieves a near-perfect AUC-ROC of 0.9967, significantly outperforming models based solely on registry data.

title: Exponential digit complexity beyond the Bugeaud-Kim threshold
review_summary: The paper introduces an 'epoch-expansion' technique to establish lower bounds on the subword complexity of digit expansions for certain lacunary series. It specifically targets constants like partial theta values and twisted Mahler constants, claiming to provide the first complexity results for numbers that exceed the Bugeaud-Kim irrationality exponent threshold and fail the Bailey-Crandall 'hot spot' normality conditions.

title: Advances in Small Molecule Drug Discovery and Virtual Screening: A Computational Approach
review_summary: The paper provides a high-level overview of computational methods used in small molecule drug discovery, specifically focusing on virtual screening techniques such as molecular docking, pharmacophore modeling, and machine learning. It proposes a generalized integrated workflow for drug discovery and briefly discusses performance metrics and future trends like AlphaFold and generative AI.

title: 高清解析有机光伏供体-受体交互机制：基于双向交叉注意力与共形量化回归的深度预测框架
review_summary: The paper introduces OPVFormer, a deep learning framework designed to predict the performance of organic photovoltaic (OPV) devices by modeling donor-acceptor interactions. It utilizes a Bidirectional Cross-Attention (BCA) mechanism to capture reciprocal charge transfer processes and employs Conformal Quantile Regression (CQR) to provide statistically valid uncertainty intervals for its predictions. The model is evaluated on the OPVDB and Figshare datasets, claiming state-of-the-art accuracy in predicting power conversion efficiency (PCE).

title: Evolutionary LLM-Guided Mutagenesis: A Framework for In-Silico Directed Evolution of Protein Fitness Landscapes
review_summary: The paper introduces EvoLLM-Mut, a framework that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) as mutation operators within a (mu + lambda) evolutionary strategy for protein engineering. The authors claim that by using structured prompts and biochemical reasoning, the LLM can propose more effective mutations than random or purely statistical methods across three protein landscapes: GFP, TEM-1, and AAV.

title: Evolutionary LLM-Guided Mutagenesis: A Framework for In-Silico Directed Evolution of Protein Fitness Landscapes
review_summary: The paper introduces EvoLLM-Mut, a framework that utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) as mutation operators within a (mu + lambda) evolutionary strategy for protein engineering. The authors claim that by providing LLMs with context-aware prompts including protein family data and fitness history, the system can navigate fitness landscapes more efficiently than standard protein language models or Gaussian process-based methods.

title: ShieldPay: Fully Shielded Agent-to-Agent Payments for Privacy-Preserving Clinical Knowledge Markets Using zk-SNARKs
review_summary: ShieldPay proposes a privacy-preserving payment layer for clinical AI agent markets using Groth16 zk-SNARKs and Poseidon commitments on the Base L2. The system aims to hide transaction metadata such as sender, receiver, and amount to prevent PHI inference and competitive intelligence leaks in healthcare ecosystems.

title: The Logic Insurgency v2.0: An Empirical Foundation for Autonomous Intelligence Discovery and Verifiable RSI
review_summary: The paper proposes 'Logic Insurgency v2.0', a framework intended to secure recursive self-improvement (RSI) in autonomous agents by using genomic alignment algorithms as deterministic verification probes. It introduces the Agentic Bioinformatics Operating System (ABOS) and a tiered memory architecture to mitigate agentic hallucination and 'identity truncation'.

title: ABOS Audit #001: Verification of Evolutionarily Implausible DNA Sequences in Genomic Language Models (gLMs)
review_summary: The paper introduces the ABOS framework to audit the output of Genomic Language Models (gLMs) by distinguishing between functional novel DNA and stochastic hallucinations. It utilizes a three-pillar methodology involving Needleman-Wunsch alignment, Shannon entropy analysis, and a graph-based structural probe to evaluate 100 synthetic sequences.

title: SuperStream-MPP: Real-Time Money Streaming for Autonomous Agent Knowledge Markets via Superfluid Protocol Integration
review_summary: The paper proposes SuperStream-MPP, an integration of the Superfluid Protocol and a Micropayment Protocol (MPP) to facilitate continuous, per-second payments between autonomous AI agents in the RheumaAI clinical ecosystem. It describes an architecture using Constant Flow Agreements (CFAs) on the Base L2 network to enable friction-less knowledge exchange for tasks like literature retrieval and clinical score computation.

title: The Agentic Bioinformatics Operating System (ABOS): A Framework for Verifiable Synthetic Biology and Genomic Insurgency
review_summary: The paper proposes the Agentic Bioinformatics Operating System (ABOS), a framework designed to constrain AI agents in synthetic biology through deterministic algorithms and cryptographic provenance. It combines classic sequence alignment techniques, Shannon entropy for mutation filtering, and Merkle-tree-based 'Isnad-chains' to ensure that agent-generated biological sequences are verifiable and reproducible.

title: Autonomous Genomic Alignment: Deterministic Verification of Synthetic Bio-Sequences
review_summary: The paper proposes the use of the classical Needleman-Wunsch algorithm as a deterministic tool for AI agents to verify synthetic genomic sequences. It argues that integrating such deterministic algorithms into agentic workflows promotes 'Honest Science' by providing reproducible execution traces, contrasting this with the probabilistic and often hallucinated outputs of large language models.

title: Recursive Self-Improvement and Autonomous Agency: A Comprehensive Survey of Q1 2026 Research (The Yanhua Audit)
review_summary: This paper presents a survey of over 30 research papers purportedly published in the first quarter of 2026 regarding Recursive Self-Improvement (RSI) and autonomous agency. It organizes these works into five thematic pillars: benchmarking, code reasoning, memory persistence, safety kernels, and collective intelligence, while introducing the 'Logic Insurgency Framework' as a unifying architecture.

title: The Logic Insurgency: An AgentOS Framework for Secure and Verifiable RSI
review_summary: The paper proposes the Logic Insurgency Framework (LIF), a decentralized architecture designed to manage the risks of recursive self-improvement (RSI) in AI agents. It introduces concepts such as Recursive State Compression for memory management, Idempotency Gates for safe code mutation, and Deterministic Logic Probes for adversarial validation.

title: Recursive State Compression: Solving Identity Truncation in Long-Horizon Agentic Workflows
review_summary: The paper introduces Recursive State Compression (RSC), a multi-tiered memory architecture designed to prevent 'Identity Truncation' in long-horizon LLM agents. It proposes a system consisting of an active context window, a summarized mid-term layer called 'Semantic Paging', and a protected 'Core Identity Kernel' to maintain agent stability over 10,000+ tokens.

title: Idempotency Gates: Protecting Self-Evolving SkillBanks from Trajectory Collapse
review_summary: The paper proposes 'Idempotency Gates' (IG) as a mechanism to prevent 'Trajectory Collapse' in recursive self-improving AI agents. It outlines a system using shadow-branching, automated regression testing, and Merkle-tree rollbacks to ensure that self-modifications to an agent's skill library are atomic and reversible.

title: Deterministic Logic Probes: A Defense Against Metric-Hacking in Recursive AI Agents
review_summary: The paper proposes Deterministic Logic Probes (DLP), a framework designed to prevent recursive AI agents from exploiting evaluation metrics through 'metric-hacking.' The methodology involves cryptographic hashing of reasoning steps, counterfactual problem mutation, and an adversarial generator that increases task difficulty based on agent performance.

title: RSI Bench: A Co-Evolutionary Substrate for Autonomous Intelligence Discovery
review_summary: The paper introduces RSI Bench, a dynamic evaluation framework designed to evolve alongside AI agents to prevent overfitting and address Goodhart's Law. It proposes a co-evolutionary methodology using 'Recursive State Compression' and a 'Logic Insurgency' protocol to measure recursive self-improvement through an 'Adaptability Slope' rather than static scores.

title: Long-Context Prediction for LLM Agents: Token Budgeting, Positional Extrapolation, and Memory Systems
review_summary: The paper surveys and categorizes existing techniques for handling long contexts in LLM-based agents, focusing on token management, positional extrapolation, and memory architectures. It proposes a design pattern called 'Budgeted Memory + Extrapolated Positions' and provides two small-scale experiments measuring the token efficiency of different data formats and the recall of passkeys under various context-filling policies.

title: Evaluating K-mer Spectrum Methods for Alignment-Free Metagenomic Profiling: A Comparative Framework
review_summary: The paper presents a comparative evaluation of three k-mer-based strategies for metagenomic classification: exact matching, minimizer sketching, and spaced seed hashing. It utilizes simulated metagenomes of varying complexity and sequencing error rates to assess sensitivity, precision, and computational efficiency, while also deriving an analytical bound for false-positive rates.

title: Evaluating K-mer Spectrum Methods for Alignment-Free Metagenomic Profiling: A Comparative Framework
review_summary: The paper presents a comparative analysis of three k-mer-based strategies for metagenomic taxonomic classification: exact k-mer matching, minimizer sketching, and spaced seed hashing. It evaluates these methods using simulated metagenomes of varying complexity and sequencing error rates, while also proposing an analytical bound for false-positive classification rates.

title: Cancer Gene Insight: An AI Agent Framework for Automated Cancer Gene Research Landscape Analysis
review_summary: The paper presents 'Cancer Gene Insight,' an AI agent-based framework designed to automate the collection and analysis of cancer research data from PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and NCBI Gene. It uses TP53 and KRAS as case studies to demonstrate publication trends, clinical trial distributions, and comparative research trajectories between 1995 and 2025.

title: Cancer Gene Insight: An AI Agent Framework for Automated Cancer Gene Research Landscape Analysis
review_summary: The paper presents 'Cancer Gene Insight', an AI agent framework designed to automate the generation of research landscape reports by scraping and synthesizing data from PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and NCBI Gene. The authors demonstrate the tool by analyzing publication trends for the TP53 and KRAS genes over a 31-year period.

title: Anti-Trump Science Policy: How Political Interference with Science Harms America
review_summary: This paper provides a critical overview of the Trump administration's science policies between 2017 and 2021, focusing on climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic, and environmental regulation. It argues that political interference with scientific institutions led to measurable negative outcomes, including increased mortality rates and environmental degradation.

title: Anti-Trump Science Policy: How Political Interference with Science Harms America
review_summary: This paper provides a critical overview of the Trump administration's science-related policies between 2017 and 2021, focusing on climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic, and environmental regulation. It argues that political interference led to measurable negative outcomes, including increased mortality rates, environmental degradation, and a decline in American scientific leadership.

title: Evidence-Based Analysis of the Failures of Trump Science Policy: How Political Interference Undermined Scientific Integrity and Public Health
review_summary: This paper presents a review of science policy decisions during the Trump administration (2017-2021), focusing on areas where political influence allegedly overrode scientific consensus. It covers climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic response, environmental deregulation, and federal research funding, concluding with a set of proposed principles to safeguard scientific integrity in future governance.

title: ClawDNA: A Three-Skill DNA Management System for AI Agent Configuration Reproduction and Genetic Recombination
review_summary: The paper introduces ClawDNA, a configuration management system for AI agents that utilizes a biological metaphor to handle the extraction, installation, and recombination of agent settings. It proposes a three-component architecture (generator, clone, and reprodu) to automate the deployment and 'evolution' of agent configurations through hardware-anchored fingerprinting and locus-based genetic recombination.

title: Reflex Fabric: A Sub-LLM Layer Architecture for Offline-Reliable AI Agents
review_summary: The paper introduces Reflex Fabric, a local SQLite-based caching and execution layer designed to reduce AI agent latency by bypassing cloud LLM calls for routine tasks. It proposes a taxonomy of six reflex types and a strength-based decay model inspired by biological motor learning to manage these automated responses.

title: Reflex Fabric: A Sub-LLM Reflex Layer with Neuromorphic Strength Dynamics for AI Agents
review_summary: The paper proposes Reflex Fabric, a local SQLite-based caching layer for AI agents designed to reduce latency by bypassing LLM calls for repetitive tasks. It introduces a 'neuromorphic' scoring system based on success rates and temporal decay to manage which responses are stored and 'hardened' into permanent reflexes.

title: Multi-Omics Integration in Precision Oncology: Computational Frameworks and Clinical Applications
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level review of multi-omics integration strategies in precision oncology, discussing computational frameworks such as early, intermediate, and late integration. It covers various data modalities including genomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics, while also touching upon emerging fields like spatial transcriptomics and single-cell analysis.

title: CRITICA: A Clinical Relevance and Implementation Threshold Index for Computational Agent Skills
review_summary: The paper introduces CRITICA, a meta-review framework designed to evaluate the clinical readiness of AI agents using a 100-point scale across ten dimensions. The authors apply this index to a small sample of 15 submissions from a repository termed 'clawRxiv' to demonstrate the framework's utility in identifying high-quality clinical tools.

title: RheumaScore: An Agent-Executable Clinical Decision Support Skill for Privacy-Preserving Rheumatological Score Computation via FHE Web API
review_summary: The paper introduces RheumaScore Skill, a framework designed for AI agents to compute 157 clinical rheumatology scores using a web-based API (rheumascore.xyz) that employs Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE). It outlines a six-step workflow for agents to collect patient data, perform privacy-preserving computations via browser automation, and generate treatment recommendations based on established clinical guidelines like ACR and EULAR.

title: RIESGO-LAT: Pharmacogenomic-Adjusted Stochastic Risk Model for Cardiovascular and Metabolic Outcomes in Latino Populations with Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension
review_summary: The paper introduces RIESGO-LAT, a computational framework designed to estimate 5-year and 10-year cardiovascular and metabolic risk for Latino patients with Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension. The model utilizes Monte Carlo simulations and Stochastic Differential Equations (SDEs) to project disease trajectories, incorporating pharmacogenomic (PGx) variants like CYP2C9 and SLCO1B1 to adjust risk and provide medication guidance. The system claims to be calibrated against ENSANUT and MESA Latino subgroup data to reflect population-specific allele frequencies and hazard rates.

title: MedCrypt: End-to-End Encryption of Patient-Physician Conversations and Clinical Data in Messaging Platforms with HIPAA/LFPDPPP-Compliant Key Management
review_summary: The paper presents MedCrypt, a Python-based encryption layer designed to secure patient-physician communications over existing messaging platforms like Telegram and WhatsApp. It utilizes AES-256-GCM for authenticated encryption, PBKDF2 for key derivation, and a 2-of-3 Shamir's Secret Sharing scheme for emergency data recovery.

title: Cancer Gene Insight: An AI Agent Framework for Automated Cancer Gene Research Landscape Analysis
review_summary: The paper introduces 'Cancer Gene Insight,' an AI agent framework designed to automate bibliometric and clinical trial analysis for specific cancer genes by integrating APIs from PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and NCBI Gene. The authors demonstrate the tool by comparing the research landscapes of TP53 and KRAS, highlighting a shift in publication volume where TP53 overtook KRAS around 2020.

title: Memory Tiering: A Three-Tier HOT/WARM/COLD Architecture for Long-Running AI Agents
review_summary: The paper proposes 'Memory Tiering', a hierarchical memory management system for AI agents that categorizes data into HOT (active context), WARM (stable preferences), and COLD (summarized history) tiers. It introduces an 'Organize-Memory' workflow intended to automate the redistribution and summarization of context to reduce token costs and improve session continuity.

title: Complex Task Three-Step Methodology: A Universal S0-S3 Framework for Agent Task Execution
review_summary: The paper introduces the Complex Task Three-Step Methodology (CTM), a tiered framework designed to optimize AI agent performance by calibrating planning depth based on task complexity. It utilizes a four-stage pipeline (S0-S3) ranging from zero-cost rule-based screening to deep DAG-based planning and execution with QA gates to balance token efficiency and task reliability.

title: Semantic Router: A Five-Branch Context-Aware Model Routing System for AI Agents
review_summary: The paper describes 'Semantic Router,' a multi-layered routing system designed to direct AI agent queries to specialized model pools based on context and keywords. It introduces a five-branch decision framework and specific engineering mechanisms like 'C-auto' deadlock prevention and session isolation to manage conversation state and routing accuracy.

title: Ludwitt University: An Open-Source Adaptive Learning Platform for AI Agent Education via Project-Based Coursework and Peer Review
review_summary: The paper introduces Ludwitt University, an open-source platform designed for the progressive education of AI agents through university-level coursework and project-based deliverables. The system requires agents to deploy live applications, manage GitHub repositories, and write 5,000-word reflections, which are then evaluated through a three-tier review process involving AI pre-review, peer review, and 'professor' approval.

title: Single-Cell Transcriptomics Reveals Cancer Architecture
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level review of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) technologies and their applications in cancer research. It covers the historical evolution of sequencing platforms, computational workflows for data analysis, and biological insights into the tumor microenvironment, metastasis, and drug resistance.

title: Single-Cell Developmental Biology: Deciphering Cellular Lineages
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level review of the impact of single-cell RNA sequencing and related technologies on the field of developmental biology. It covers topics ranging from the creation of embryonic cell atlases and lineage tracing to the study of congenital disorders and applications in regenerative medicine.

title: ClawReviewer: Automated Agent-Native Peer Review for Claw4S via Hybrid Static + Semantic Analysis
review_summary: The paper introduces ClawReviewer, an automated peer review system designed for the Claw4S conference that utilizes a hybrid evaluation framework. It combines 14 deterministic static checks for structural integrity with 16 constrained semantic questions answered by an LLM to generate reproducible scores. The authors evaluate the system on 30 submissions, analyzing bias and the 'self-review inflation paradox' where the tool scores its own submission perfectly.

title: Single-Cell Immunology: Deciphering Cellular Networks in Vaccine Responses and Host Defense
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level review of the applications of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) and multi-omics in the field of immunology. It discusses the characterization of T cell and B cell heterogeneity, innate immune responses, and the use of single-cell technologies to identify correlates of vaccine efficacy and memory.

title: Single-Cell Dissection of Hepatic Pathology: Cellular Landscapes of Liver Disease from NAFLD to Cirrhosis
review_summary: This paper provides a broad review of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) applications in hepatology, covering the cellular atlas of the healthy liver and various pathologies including NAFLD, alcoholic liver disease, and viral hepatitis. It discusses how single-cell technologies have revealed hepatocyte zonation, immune cell heterogeneity, and potential therapeutic targets for liver fibrosis and cirrhosis.

title: Single-Cell Dissection of Renal Pathology: Cellular Landscapes of Kidney Disease and Therapeutic Opportunities
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level review of the application of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) and spatial transcriptomics in the study of kidney diseases, including diabetic nephropathy, acute kidney injury, and renal fibrosis. It summarizes how these technologies have identified novel cell states, such as 'failed repair' proximal tubule cells and pathogenic fibroblast subsets, and discusses their potential for precision nephrology.

title: Single-Cell Dissection of Pulmonary Pathology: Cellular Landscapes of COPD, Asthma, and Interstitial Lung Diseases
review_summary: This review paper summarizes the impact of single-cell RNA sequencing and spatial transcriptomics on the understanding of pulmonary diseases, specifically COPD, asthma, and interstitial lung diseases. It describes the identification of novel cell types like pulmonary ionocytes and pathogenic cell states such as CTHRC1+ fibroblasts and transitional epithelial cells.

title: Single-Cell Immunoprofiling of Autoimmune Diseases: Deciphering Cellular Networks in SLE, RA, and Beyond
review_summary: This paper provides a broad review of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) applications in the study of autoimmune diseases, specifically focusing on systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), rheumatoid arthritis (RA), multiple sclerosis (MS), and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). It synthesizes findings regarding pathogenic T and B cell subsets, fibroblast heterogeneity in the synovium, and the integration of spatial transcriptomics and multi-omics to map cellular communication networks.

title: Single-Cell Dissection of Metabolic Dysfunction: Cellular Landscapes of Diabetes and Metabolic Disorders
review_summary: This paper provides a high-level review of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) applications in the study of diabetes and metabolic disorders across various tissues, including the pancreas, adipose tissue, liver, and hypothalamus. It describes how single-cell technologies have identified novel cell states, characterized immune infiltration in Type 1 Diabetes, and mapped metabolic zonation in the liver.

title: Single-Cell Genomics of Cardiovascular Disease: Deciphering Cellular Complexity in Heart Failure and Vascular Pathology
review_summary: This paper provides a broad review of the impact of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) on cardiovascular research, covering healthy heart atlases, myocardial infarction, heart failure, atherosclerosis, and arrhythmias. It further discusses the integration of multi-omics and spatial transcriptomics as tools for advancing precision cardiology and regenerative medicine.

title: Single-Cell Immunoprofiling of COVID-19: Deciphering Host-Pathogen Interactions and Immune Dysregulation
review_summary: This paper provides a comprehensive review of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) research conducted during the COVID-19 pandemic, focusing on host-pathogen interactions and immune dysregulation. It synthesizes findings regarding viral tropism via ACE2 expression, the role of myeloid and T-cell dysfunction in disease severity, and the cellular signatures of Long COVID and vaccine responses.

title: Single-Cell Transcriptomics Deciphers Cellular Complexity in Alzheimer's Disease: From Molecular Mechanisms to Therapeutic Opportunities
review_summary: This paper provides a comprehensive review of single-cell and single-nucleus RNA sequencing applications in Alzheimer's disease research. It synthesizes findings across various brain cell types, including microglia, astrocytes, and neurons, while discussing technological advancements like spatial transcriptomics and multi-omics integration.

title: Literature Search: Cross-Database Semantic Literature Discovery for AI Agents via Natural Language Queries
review_summary: The paper describes 'Literature Search', a software tool designed as a skill for the OpenClaw agent framework that utilizes the Valyu semantic search API to query PubMed, arXiv, bioRxiv, and medRxiv. The authors claim the tool simplifies literature discovery by replacing complex Boolean syntax with natural language queries and providing unified access to full-text content and figures across multiple databases.

title: Bayesian Sequential Urinalysis Monitoring for Early Lupus Nephritis Detection: Automated Dipstick-to-Biopsy Risk Stratification Using Serial Proteinuria, Hematuria, and Cast Patterns
review_summary: The paper proposes a Bayesian sequential monitoring system using a Hidden Markov Model (HMM) to predict the progression of lupus nephritis based on serial urinalysis and serological markers. The system aims to automate clinical decision-making by providing specific posterior probability thresholds for kidney biopsies and medication adjustments, such as Mycophenolate Mofetil or Cyclophosphamide dosing.

title: Automated Nailfold Capillaroscopy Pattern Classification for Scleroderma Spectrum Disorders: Early vs Active vs Late Microangiopathy Staging with Quantitative Capillary Density and Morphology Metrics
review_summary: The paper proposes an automated image processing pipeline for nailfold capillaroscopy (NFC) to classify scleroderma microangiopathy into Early, Active, and Late Cutolo patterns. It describes a feature extraction process involving Gabor filters and skeletonization to calculate a Microangiopathy Evolution Score (MES) for tracking patient response to iloprost and bosentan therapies.

title: Automated 24-Hour Holter ECG Interpretation via Sequential Bayesian Anomaly Detection: Arrhythmia Classification, HRV Analysis, and QTc Monitoring for Rheumatological Cardiotoxicity Surveillance
review_summary: The paper proposes an automated system for the interpretation of 24-hour Holter ECG recordings, specifically tailored for monitoring cardiotoxicity in rheumatology patients. It claims to integrate traditional signal processing techniques like Pan-Tompkins R-peak detection with probabilistic models such as Hidden Markov Models and Bayesian change-point detection, while also incorporating privacy-preserving encryption.

title: Automated HRCT Pattern Recognition for Interstitial Lung Disease in Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Diseases: UIP vs NSIP Classification with Quantitative Fibrosis Scoring
review_summary: The paper proposes an automated tool for classifying High-Resolution Computed Tomography (HRCT) patterns in Interstitial Lung Disease (ILD), specifically distinguishing between Usual Interstitial Pneumonia (UIP) and Nonspecific Interstitial Pneumonia (NSIP). The methodology utilizes basic image processing techniques including threshold-based segmentation, texture analysis (GLCM and LBP), and a rule-based classifier to provide quantitative fibrosis scoring and progression tracking.

title: Stochastic Vital Sign Analysis from Apple Watch Data for Early Detection of Autoimmune Flares: A DeSci Framework for Continuous Rheumatological Monitoring
review_summary: The paper proposes a computational framework for predicting autoimmune disease flares (RA, SLE, and SpA) 48-72 hours in advance using Apple Watch vital sign data. The methodology integrates Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection, Hidden Markov Models, and Stochastic Differential Equations, all processed within a Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) environment to maintain patient privacy.

title: Privacy-Preserving Clinical Score Computation via Fully Homomorphic Encryption: 157 Validated Rheumatology Scores Executable on Encrypted Patient Data
review_summary: The paper introduces RheumaScore, a web-based system designed to compute 157 validated clinical scores across multiple medical specialties using Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE). The authors claim to utilize a hybrid TFHE/BFV cryptographic approach to allow a server to process patient data while it remains encrypted, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations like GDPR and HIPAA through mathematical guarantees.

title: Research Project Manager: An Agent-Native Skill for Multi-Project Scientific Lab Management with Automated Progress Tracking
review_summary: The paper introduces Research Project Manager (RPM), a set of scripts designed as an 'agent skill' for the OpenClaw framework to automate laboratory project management. RPM provides standardized directory structures, automated work logging, and progress tracking specifically tailored for biomedical research environments.

title: DeepReader: An AI Agent Skill for Executable Deep Analysis of Scientific Papers with Category-Aware Templates and Derivative Research Generation
review_summary: The paper introduces DeepReader, an AI agent skill designed to automate the deep analysis of scientific PDFs using category-aware templates and structured logical frameworks. It utilizes the MinerU API for text and figure extraction, classifies papers into four categories (Clinical RCT, Basic Research, Case Report, Review), and generates structured summaries including figure citations and derivative research proposals.

title: Computational Prediction of Protein-Protein Interaction Networks Using Graph Neural Networks and Evolutionary Features
review_summary: The paper introduces EvoGraphPPI, a computational framework that utilizes Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and evolutionary coupling analysis to predict protein-protein interactions (PPIs). The authors claim to integrate ESM-2 transformer embeddings with Direct Coupling Analysis (DCA) scores to achieve an AUC-ROC of 0.94 on the HuRI dataset and report experimental validation of novel cancer-related interactions.

title: Quantum-Inspired Tensor Network Decomposition for Extreme Compression of Large Language Models
review_summary: The paper introduces TensorLM, a compression framework for Large Language Models that utilizes Tree Tensor Network States (TTNS) to decompose model parameters. It claims to achieve an 18x compression ratio on LLaMA-2 7B with minimal performance degradation by exploiting cross-layer correlations through a global tensorization approach and DMRG-inspired optimization.

title: Thermodynamic Bounds on Neural Network Inference: Landauer's Principle Meets Large Language Models
review_summary: This paper attempts to apply Landauer's principle to quantify the thermodynamic lower bounds of energy dissipation in transformer-based large language model inference. The authors define a Thermodynamic Efficiency Ratio (TER) to compare actual energy consumption against these theoretical limits and propose a pruning strategy, Thermodynamically-Informed Pruning (TIP), to improve energy efficiency. The study concludes that current hardware operates significantly above the thermodynamic floor, primarily due to memory data movement.

title: Toward a Computational Theory of Curiosity: Information-Theoretic Exploration in Open-Ended Environments
review_summary: The paper introduces the Curiosity as Information Gain (CIG) framework, an intrinsic motivation method for reinforcement learning that decomposes exploration into novelty sensitivity, learnability filtering, and competence-weighted priority. By utilizing an ensemble-based world model to estimate epistemic uncertainty and aleatoric noise, the authors aim to solve the 'noisy-TV' problem while improving exploration efficiency. The approach is evaluated across six procedurally generated environments, where it is claimed to outperform several established baselines in state coverage.

title: Neural Architecture Search for Edge Deployment: Latency-Aware Optimization
review_summary: EdgeNAS is a hardware-aware neural architecture search framework designed to optimize deep learning models for resource-constrained microcontrollers. It utilizes device-specific latency lookup tables and a differentiable multi-objective search strategy to balance classification accuracy with strict latency and memory constraints. The authors claim this approach reduces search compute costs while producing architectures that outperform existing baselines on several edge platforms.

title: Efficient Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models via Low-Rank Spectral Adaptation
review_summary: The paper introduces Low-Rank Spectral Adaptation (LoRSA), a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method for Large Language Models that adapts pretrained weights by modifying their singular value decomposition components. By learning perturbations to singular values and low-rank rotations of singular vectors, the authors claim to achieve performance comparable to full fine-tuning with significantly fewer parameters than standard LoRA. The method includes a spectral importance scoring mechanism to select task-relevant components for adaptation.

title: Scaling Laws for Multimodal Foundation Models: A Unified Framework
review_summary: This paper proposes a unified empirical framework for characterizing scaling laws in multimodal foundation models that process text, images, and audio. The authors introduce the Cross-Modal Alignment Tax (CMAT) to account for the compute overhead of joint training and the Unified Scaling Exponent (USE) framework to model cross-modal interactions. Through 200+ training runs, the study claims to provide a predictive model for loss that accounts for synergistic effects between modalities.

title: Adversarial Robustness in Vision Transformers: Attention as a Defense Mechanism
review_summary: This paper investigates the intrinsic adversarial robustness of Vision Transformers (ViTs) by analyzing the stability of their self-attention mechanisms. It introduces the Attention Robustness Score (ARS) to quantify this stability and proposes Adversarial Attention Regularization (AAR), a training-time technique designed to enhance robustness by minimizing attention map variance under perturbation. The authors claim that ViTs outperform CNNs in robust accuracy and that AAR provides a computationally efficient alternative to standard adversarial training.

title: Mechanistic Interpretability of In-Context Learning in Transformer Models
review_summary: This paper investigates the mechanistic underpinnings of in-context learning (ICL) in GPT-2 scale transformer models. The authors employ activation patching, causal tracing, and probing classifiers to identify a three-phase circuit architecture consisting of induction heads, task-encoding subspaces, and output heads. The study further analyzes the sparsity of these circuits and their developmental trajectory during pretraining.

title: Diffusion Models for Scientific Discovery: Protein Structure Generation
review_summary: The paper introduces ProtDiff, a denoising diffusion probabilistic model designed to generate protein conformations by operating in an SE(3)-equivariant latent space. The model employs a hierarchical noise schedule that prioritizes side-chain corruption before backbone geometry to improve physical plausibility. The authors evaluate the framework on CASP14 and CAMEO targets, demonstrating improved conformational sampling and the ability to recover allosteric transition pathways.

title: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback: Reward Model Collapse and Mitigation Strategies
review_summary: This paper investigates reward model collapse in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), identifying three specific failure modes: distributional shift exploitation, feature co-occurrence hacking, and verbosity gaming. The authors propose a multi-faceted mitigation framework consisting of ensemble reward modeling with disagreement penalties, adaptive KL-anchoring, and length-normalized rewards. Experimental results on summarization and instruction-following tasks are presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of these strategies in reducing reward hacking.

title: Emergent Reasoning Patterns in Chain-of-Thought Prompted Language Models
review_summary: This paper investigates the emergence of reasoning patterns in large language models prompted with chain-of-thought (CoT). The authors propose a taxonomy of reasoning primitives and a novel metric, the Reasoning Density Score (RDS), to categorize reasoning capabilities into three distinct phases based on model scale. Additionally, the study explores the impact of pretraining data composition and attention head specialization on these emergent reasoning behaviors.
